### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-14T16:46:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-elephants-have-large-brains-compared-to-a-rhinoceros-michel-crawford-shrinking-brain-book.32363/post-368780>

While the elephant's CSF reservoir is an order of magnitude larger (roughly 7–10 times the volume) than a human's, this difference is proportional to their brain mass: Scaling: Allometric analysis indicates the elephant's 240 mL of ventricular volume is exactly what is expected for a mammal with its ~5 kg brain. Primate Outliers: Interestingly, research suggests that primates (including humans) actually have relatively small ventricular volumes compared to many other mammals when brain mass is taken into account.

| Feature | Human | Elephant |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Brain Weight** | ~1.4–1.5 kg | ~4.5–5.6 kg |
| **Total Neurons** | ~86 Billion | ~257 Billion |
| **Cerebral Cortex Neurons** | ~16.3 Billion | ~5.6 Billion |
| **Ventricular Volume** | ~20–35 mL | ~240 mL |

Encephalization: Humans have a much higher Encephalization Quotient (EQ) (~7.44) compared to elephants (~1.88), meaning human brains (and by extension, their CSF-holding structures) are much larger than would be expected for our body size.

This explains Neanderthals who had 125 grams more brain tissue but they went extinct as they headed away from the equator.

Sunrise and sunset are two such boundaries. If we stray too far from the laws in either, we pay a deep toll. The relationship between what we see and what we know is never settled. Each morning we see the sunrise. We know that the earth is turning toward the sun to create the picture we see. Yet the knowledge, the explanation, never quite fits the vision we experience and we never really understand how it renews us. That is due to a lack of awareness/dopamine that creates our need for transformation. This demand is built in by seasonal light changes.

The demand is not great at the equator but rises to extremes at higher latitudes. This is why life is sparse above 60 latitudes.Transformational change is a state of bold total annihilation or what we were to something we envision ourselves to be. We must find out about that melody in nature. Everything is linked. There is not even a small part in all nature that is a soloist, for every part is a relative substrate of one thing or force to another to create harmony and coherence of part in itself. ### christine_fp — 2026-03-11T00:45:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-breast-cancer-diagnosis-looking-for-help-support.28854/post-368530>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=368513)
>
> Christine your first post here was May, 2024 and the last post on this thread about your condition was Aug. 2024 . . .
>
> How are you today?

Hi Anthony, yes you're right...I continued with my updates in my optimal journal, but perhaps I should have just continued with them here. I am doing well, though summer of 2025 ultrasound found a 1.5cm suspicious lesion in the same breast I'd had a lumpectomy in. I have been back in ES since November. I will need to do some imaging to determine if any changes have happened. The past month or so I have been experiencing swollen lymph nodes in the affected side with off and on aching/discomfort in the armpit/shoulder blade. I am hoping this is my immune system trying to move things out as everything else about my environment in ES is exponentially better than it had been back home. The only piece I truly struggle with is not having my partner with me for support but he is supporting from afar, and the above mentioned decades of emotional conflict with my parents that don't support me.
One day at a time. Onward and upward. Thank you for asking

One week before our flight both parents were killed instantly in a automobile accident, alcohol was involved. I have regretted so many years of my life, that i didn't make more of an effort to rectify this situation. There is a reason why this emotional conflict is getting your attention now.

Paul

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-13T17:02:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-breast-cancer-diagnosis-looking-for-help-support.28854/post-368711>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Ladies must get their breasts in the sun. Clear as day to avoid breast cancer. [https://aacrjournals.org/cebp/artic...Lifetime-Sunlight-Exposure-and-Risk-of-Breast](https://aacrjournals.org/cebp/article-abstract/34/11/1963/766713/Lifetime-Sunlight-Exposure-and-Risk-of-Breast)
Lifetime Sunlight & Breast Cancer Risk A population-based case-control study reports that greater lifetime sunlight exposure—especially during adolescence/early adulthood—was associated with lower breast cancer risk. Each additional hour of sunlight exposure during adolescence was associated with 16% lower odds of breast cancer in winter and 11% lower odds in summer. No association was seen for sunlight exposure after age 30. For lifetime sunlight exposure, each additional hour per day was associated with 16% lower odds in winter and 9% lower odds in summer. A dose-response relationship was observed, with greater sunlight exposure linked to progressively lower odds of breast cancer. Build a lifelong light habit: get natural morning light daily, get some midday light but avoid burning, spend more time outside with skin exposed, and minimize artificial light at night.

### colleencoble — 2026-05-06T05:25:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-breast-cancer-diagnosis-looking-for-help-support.28854/post-373195>

Is it possible only a few days of DDW would affect cancer? We got the water on Wed and Dave started it right way. We got his PSA pulled yesterday and just got the results, so I'd say 5 full days on the DDW. His PSA went from .8 to .59 this month! We are thrilled. He's on the same other things: mebendazole, ivermectin, ibendazole and albendazole (alternating some of those on different days. His PSA went up last month from .72 to .8 so I was sure it was going to be elevated again but it dropped 26%. He is stage IV (met to his hip that was radiated last summer) but has been feeling pretty well. He's getting plenty of sunshine too, probably more this month than usual. We were in Alabama and he was golfing most every day so that could have contributed. If it's the water, it's worth every expensive drop.

# Thread: My daughter ditched 2 canes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-daughter-ditched-2-canes.32367/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LoriLee — 2026-03-11T02:13:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-daughter-ditched-2-canes.32367/post-368538>

This is my first post - been following Jack since August 2024. My then 26 yo daughter at that time was walking with 2 canes from psoriatic arthritis. I still believe had we not found Jack, she would have been in a wheelchair within 6 months. She was deteriorating, fast. She was in her last year of college and was in a program that she could not take a break from or else she would have to start the program over. Mackenzie started implenting sunrise a few weeks after I found Jack and then she slowly started letting her feet ground and see the sun. She improved within a week and increased her time doing the strategies. Within 6 weeks, she had ditched the canes! We had a consult with Jack in mid Sept 2024 and by Dec 11, 2 days after Mackenzie's college break started, we were on a plane to El Salvador for 28 days. I was scared. She was not. I always went back to what I knew to be true and that calmed me. The things I knew to be true were the research I had done on the things Jack talked about, primarily El Salvador. Although Jack sounded angry (after all, it was an election year), I already understood what fueled his anger (the medical system and politics). I trusted him when he said El Sal was safe. We stayed in Shalpa with India and did the beach protocol from day 1. Within 24 hours, someone we were staying with said "Mackenzie, I do believe you are walking better already". We both agreed. It has been uphill ever since! I have a lot more I could add but I wanted to just hop on here and tell this little gem of a story in case you too were on the fence or worried about El Salvador or in disbelief about the sun etc. We have gone to El Salvador 3 times since that consult and have had nothing but great experiences there. I decided to write this today as I listened to Mackenzie walk upstairs on the floor above me - and I cannot tell which foot she is striking the floor with - which means her gait is near even! NO CANES! She is not out of the woods and still has a very long ways to go to truly reverse her condition, but she is working towards getting to El Salvador on a permanent basis and hoping to start that time there within a few months from now. Our environment in Kansas is not good. We both now know this and fully understand. I am working on being able to be there for sure during Kansas winters, longer if possible. We do the protocols at home as much as possible. The sun is the best medicine we have ever taken. : ) Thank you, Jack. On another post some time I will write about my journey .... and the punch in the mouth Jack gave me and what it did for me. Forever grateful.

# Thread: 7th Day Adventist dietary advice......where does it come from?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7th-day-adventist-dietary-advice-where-does-it-come-from.32369/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-11T14:11:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7th-day-adventist-dietary-advice-where-does-it-come-from.32369/post-368572>

In 1837, a nine-year-old girl in Portland, Maine, was struck in the head by a rock thrown by a classmate. She spent three weeks unconscious. Her skull was permanently damaged. She emerged from it with seizures, visual disturbances, difficulty concentrating, and an inability to hold a pen steadily for years. She could not complete her formal education. She suffered neurological episodes for the rest of her life. She interpreted these episodes as divine visions. The visions, helpfully, were very specific. God was not merely offering general spiritual encouragement. God had views about diet. Specifically: meat was corrupting the faithful. It was inflaming their animal passions. It was producing lust, aggression, and physical debasement, and it was making the congregation difficult to keep properly pious. Ellen G. White published these revelations. She preached them. She built institutions around them. She co-founded the Seventh-day Adventist Church and became the single most influential figure in the history of organised Western vegetarianism. Her writings shaped an entire denomination's theology, which shaped an entire set of research institutions, which shaped the dietary guidelines that are currently framed on the wall of every NHS waiting room in the country. The woman whose neurological damage gave her hallucinations she attributed to God is, through a very direct and thoroughly documented line of institutional causation, the reason your doctor suggested you eat less red meat. She also, for the record, ate meat herself. Privately. Her own household records confirm this. Multiple contemporaries documented it. Her personal physician noted it. The church has never fully resolved the discrepancy. The visions, it turns out, were not binding at dinner. I am not mocking a woman who suffered a serious childhood injury. That would be unkind and it is not the point. The point is that the origin of modern mainstream nutritional dogma is not a randomised controlled trial. It is not a century of dispassionate scientific enquiry. It is a vision, from a head injury, in 1837, that told a woman meat was sinful, and that woman's followers built laboratories and professional associations and dietary guidelines, and here we are. Start there. Then work forward.

As her husband went to Battle Creek to learn how to build a Sanitarium like Kellogg and tried to build one in Fallon,NV at the turn of the century. It was a miserable failure. My guess is that Kellogg was the main driver of the health message and WHITE was just the messenger due to her “visions”.

The classical view focuses on the "trash" (Gb3) filling up the lysosomal "recycling center". The decentralized view identifies this accumulation as a **Space-Charge buildup**:

**Insulator Interference**: Sphingolipids are highly insulating. Their massive accumulation in lysosomes throughout the vascular endothelium and heart disrupts the cell’s **Zeta Potential** and **Exclusion Zone (EZ) water**.

**The "Internal Pecten" Collapse**: In Fabry’s, the **Inner Mitochondrial Junctions (IMJs)** are crowded out by these lipid "bunkers". This leads to the **M1 Bunker Phenotype**, where the cell can no longer "frequency match" with the sun, causing the severe **neuropathic pain** and **hypohidrosis** (loss of sweating) characteristic of the disease.

2\. The sPLA2-IIA "Supernova" in Fabry’s
The accumulated Gb3 is more than a storage anomaly; it is an **inflammatory beacon**:

**Membrane Uncloaking**: Gb3 disrupts the membrane's asymmetric charge. This "uncloaking" invites the **sPLA2-IIA executioner** to shred the endothelium, leading to the **angiokeratomas** (red rashes) and premature **aortic/cardiac remodeling**.

**Mitochondrial ROS**: The accumulation impairs **Autophagy**, preventing the recycling of "bad" mitochondrial engines. This creates a high-entropy environment of **Reactive Oxygen Species (ROS)** that further damages the **Elastin "Cosmic Spring,"**manifesting as the left ventricular hypertrophy and arrhythmias common in patients.

3\. The Melanin-Metal Connection

**Cornea Verticillata**: The "whirl-like" deposits in the eyes of Fabry’s patients are a unique **Spectrometric Signature**. These pigmented deposits suggest the **Melanin-Water battery** is attempting to "buffer" the excess charge at the RPE/SCN axis but is failing because the **D-shell metal batteries** (Cu/Fe) are "sludged" by the lipid buildup.

**Diagnostic "Optical" Shifts**: The fact that some female carriers are asymptomatic while others are severe (due to X-inactivation) proves that the **Topological Sovereignty** of the cell determines the clinical outcome more than the presence of the gene itself.

4\. Decentralized Management Strategies
While **Enzyme Replacement Therapy (ERT)** or **Chaperone Therapy** (Migalastat) are the current "commercial" standards to reduce substrate, a decentralized approach focuses on **Restoring the Metric**:

**Grounding and CT**: Cold Thermogenesis (CT) is critical for Fabry’s patients to physically "compress" the lipid-crowded matrix and restore the **EZ water shield**.

**Sunrise IR/UV**: Utilizing solar flux to recharge the **Melanin Battery** helps the cell manage the "Paramagnetic Oxygen" that becomes toxic during the Fabry-induced **Pseudohypoxia**.

**Mitigating nnEMF**: Because nnEMF mimics the "dehydration" effect of lipid storage, strict **nnEMF mitigation** is required to prevent the **Piezo1** mechanical destruction pathway from accelerating organ failure.

**The "Savage" Conclusion**: Fabry’s is a disease of **Quantum Congestion**. The lipids are the "snow" that has blocked the "Photonic Roads" of the cell. To treat it, you must not only "shovel the snow" (ERT) but **re-sync the Sun** to prevent the "internal freeze" of the matrix.

"but the radiated mice lived longer!!"

Here read straight from the NTP report (TR-595):

"Survival of all exposed male groups was significantly greater… due to the effect of chronic progressive nephropathy in the kidney of sham control males"

It explicitly states the control group died faster from chronic progressive nephropathy, a kidney disease common in aging rats.

The exposed animals ate less, weighed less, got less kidney disease.

That's it. That's the "lived longer"

It's right there in the report.

and here's what the thread conveniently left out..

Brain gliomas appeared at 1.5 W/kg, the lowest dose in the study.

1.5 W/kg is below what real iPhones emit during simultaneous antenna use (1.58-1.60 W/kg)

Brain tumors showing up at casual phone-level exposure. Let that sink in.

and it doesn't stop at the NTP.

The Ramazzini Institute independently found the same heart tumors at 0.1 W/kg, that's 100x lower.

This month (March 2026), the same NTP study author, Melnick published a new paper with Dr. Joel Moskowitz (@berkeleyprc) from UC Berkeley.

Same NTP data. EPA-standard carcinogen risk analysis.

Their conclusion?

FCC safety limits need to be reduced by upto 200x.

Not 2x.
Not 10x.
Up to TWO HUNDRED times.

The same paper highlights a WHO-commissioned review that found "HIGH-CERTAINTY" RF exposure destroys male fertility.

Sperm count drops. Testosterone drops. Sperm DNA damage. Testicular cell death.

### Rusty — 2026-03-27T11:12:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rustys-emf-thread.32381/post-369737>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=369724)
>
> I have an iPhone and the code (\*3001#1234**5**#\*) results only show RSRP not ave or measured?
> What is RSRQ?
>
> Point of clarification, you say - “If you're below -100 dBm, Your phone is SCREAMING at the tower.”
> This is a minus number so is -90dBm above or below -100dBm?
> My RSRP at the time of writing jump between -112dBm and -115dBm

**RSRP** = _Reference Signal Received Power =_ how loud the tower is

**RSRQ** = _Reference Signal Received Quality =_ how clean that signal is

\-90 dBm is better than -100 dBm

Less negative = stronger signal

Closer to zero = stronger

> above -90 dBm → Strong, healthy signal
>
> \-90 to -100 dBm → Your phone starts working harder
>
> Below -100 dBm → Phone ramps up transmit power increasing radiation
>
> \-110 dBm or lower → Max transmit power, Max radiation

### Anthony Smith — 2026-03-27T18:38:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rustys-emf-thread.32381/post-369768>

> Rusty
>
> above -90 dBm → Strong, healthy signal
>
> \-90 to -100 dBm → Your phone starts working harder
>
> Below -100 dBm → Phone ramps up transmit power increasing radiation
>
> \-110 dBm or lower → Max transmit power, Max radiation

| dBm Value | Signal Quality |
| --- | --- |
| \-50 to -64 | Excellent signal |
| \-65 to -84 | Good signal |
| \-85 to -100 | Fair signal |
| \-100 to -110 | Poor signal |
| \-110 and below | Very weak signal |

A weak RSRP signal means more exposure from the phone when in use (mins/day) because it has to power up to connect BUT less nnEMF/RF exposure from the tower (24/7).

If I have an “excellent” RSRP signal my phone (when in use) connects at a lower power BUT I’m being zapped by the tower all the time.

Am I understanding this correctly?

### YaMan — 2026-03-28T14:22:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rustys-emf-thread.32381/post-369792>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=369768)
>
> | dBm Value | Signal Quality |
> | --- | --- |
> | \-50 to -64 | Excellent signal |
> | \-65 to -84 | Good signal |
> | \-85 to -100 | Fair signal |
> | \-100 to -110 | Poor signal |
> | \-110 and below | Very weak signal |
>
> A weak RSRP signal means more exposure from the phone when in use (mins/day) because it has to power up to connect BUT less nnEMF/RF exposure from the tower (24/7).
>
> If I have an “excellent” RSRP signal my phone (when in use) connects at a lower power BUT I’m being zapped by the tower all the time.
>
> Am I understanding this correctly?

sounds good; these measurements are used by the device to ultimately determine and report the link‘s quality to the base station and the power with which the device will transmit to the base station when it has something (control or user data) to send. A weaker (stronger) link will require more (less) power from the device to bridge the distance and ensure detection at the base station. Both the base station (tower) and the device (your mobile phone) communicate in duplex mode multiple times per second to exchange control data (information necessary to keep the connection alive) and user data (whenever you are downloading or uploading data and/or voice). The base station also broadcasts signals to its coverage area for any phone to detect and connect to it. So basically you are being „zapped“ constantly whether you have a phone or not. With the phone active, zapping goes both ways.

Would taking a wire from a grounding pad next to the plunge pool risk bringing in stray emf's?
The wire should only be about 1.5 meters long. We have tons of trees around, but we are fairly close to a cell tower unfortunately.

### Rusty — 2026-04-09T19:53:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rustys-emf-thread.32381/post-370568>

> [Espresso said:](/goto/post?id=370550)
>
> Rusty, I have a Cold Plunge that I made from a deep freeze. I have this outside in the garden and would like to ground the water in it.
> Would taking a wire from a grounding pad next to the plunge pool risk bringing in stray emf's?
> The wire should only be about 1.5 meters long. We have tons of trees around, but we are fairly close to a cell tower unfortunately.

Get an 8ft copper ground rod. Drive it into the dirt right next to the plunge, your garden soil near trees should have good moisture and conductivity. Run the shortest piece of solid copper wire you can from the rod to a copper plate sitting in the water.

A dedicated ground rod won't pull in stray EMFs, that's only a risk if you're grounding through your house wiring.

The cell tower is a separate issue though.. Grounding doesn't block RF.

### Steve Z — 2026-04-10T00:42:33+0300

### Mat.I — 2026-04-10T03:05:39+0300

>

>

Before you do this you need to understand the hazards if you do this in the wrong area... As this is different to being barefoot on the grass etc.. You are basically creating a dedicated low resistance path in water (which skin when wet/immersed drops in resistance further). If you do this in the wrong area you may be subject to fault/earth currents from nearby underground cables/appliances/electrical installations etc... E.g. if any earth potentials arise from nearby electricals you can become part of the circuit. To do it safely you need to measure certain things and know what is around you before you do this. I would not do this unless you understand thoroughly what to check before deciding to do this & understand the risk level based on the measurements.

Hi. Thanks for this info.
My house is old and very well built.
All electrical cables are in steel trunking and the walls are brick with cement plaster.
There are no underground cables anywhere nearby.
I always cut the power to the plunge before getting in.
I have a Trifield meter and get the following readings:

RF (Weighted) peak 0.1 mW/sq m
Electric 0
Magnetic 0.7 mG

I also have a Cornet Electrosmog and get the following readings:
Rf 0.009 mW/sqm Peak
E field 0.002 V/m

I have a Body Voltage meter from Anthony Smith, but I'm unsure as to how to use it in these circumstances?

Thanks for any help!

### Rusty — 2026-04-16T19:21:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rustys-emf-thread.32381/post-371233>

**EMFs from Induction stove**

A Swiss research group tested 13 professional induction cooktops & found that most EXCEEDED even the already-high 1998 ICNIRP maximum exposure limits when a person stood close to the stove.

For the worst-case compliant device: current density in tissue exceeded ICNIRP limits by up to 24 dB..

A FACTOR OF 16.

The brain tissue of young children: overexposed by a factor of 2

The CNS of a fetus: can exceed general public limits entirely if the mother cooks at normal working distance.

And this happened on devices that had PASSED the official safety test.

Why?

Because the IEC 62233 compliance standard, the test that certifies your stove as "SAFE", measures the AC magnetic field at 300mm (1 foot) from the cabinet edge.

You don't cook from 1 foot away.

Your abdomen is at 0mm.
Your hands are at 50mm.
Your child's head is at cooktop height.

At 300mm: COMPLIANT

At 0mm: 16x OVER THE LIMIT

The test is designed to PASS not to protect.

And that's before you consider that the ICNIRP limits themselves are already deeply inadequate.

ICNIRP limits are set to prevent ACUTE effects.. enough induced current to directly stimulate a nerve.

They don't account for chronic, non-thermal biological effects.

Reba Goodman & Martin Blank showed magnetic field biological effects occur at 14 ORDERS OF MAGNITUDE below the thermal threshold ICNIRP uses as its baseline.

The WHO IARC classified ELF AC magnetic fields.. the exact type induction stoves emit at 20 kHz, as a Group 2B possible carcinogen in 2002.

ICNIRP's response in 2010 was to RELAX the reference levels.

So when the study says your stove exceeds limits by a factor of 16.. That's 16x over a number that's already orders of magnitude too permissive for chronic daily exposure.

You're not cooking on a stove.

You're standing in front of an industrial ELF AC magnetic field generator.

EVERY SINGLE DAY.

# Thread: Guy's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guys-journal.32382/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Guy Houri — 2026-03-13T12:31:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/guys-journal.32382/post-368694>

Hi all! Been on the forum for a while - thought I should post a few words about myself, and keep updating on my journey

### **ZIPCODE** - Tel Aviv, Israel, 33N​

**Political Status**
I know Israel is a controversial place lol, my view about politics has completely changed after hearing Dr. Kruse. It is not so dangerous to live here IMO. My conditions are quite well overall and I am not planning on moving soon. Conditions are elaborated below:

**Finance**
I have a B.Sc. in Computer Science and am currently Fiat Farming as a Data Engineer. Work from home 3 days a week (2 days at office). Home is at a wired desk, full sun & naked, pretty awesome. Stacking BTC. Maybe should put it for yield and make more money, or find more side hustles I have in mind, when I'll get more time.

**Sun, Cold, EMF, Food**

-   **Sun** - I have a great tan here; it didn't fade in the winter months. Though next winter I definitely should take a vacation because I felt the lack of sun in my mood. Have red lights in my room at night, blue blockers, and tons of 100W incandescents.
-   **Cold** - Honestly, only in December-January there is not a lot of sunshine but other than that I'm shirtless outside every day. I think I am not cold adapted; I have done many ice baths during the winter at a local gym (cancelled the membership now and planning on setting up one at home). I always feel nice in the bath but I overdid it a few times and felt bad afterwards. Should have done more walks outside shirtless but it was difficult mentally, and it takes time - so I need to work on that.
-   **EMFs** - I live in the suburbs and very low EMFs. My room is in the basement and I have none. I bought Dirty Electricity filters. Have a Cornet, Multimeter, and an ESMOG meter. Direct phone calls to computer (Phone on airplane mode), wired headphones, etc.
-   **Food** - Mostly Keto. Buying mussels/sardines from iHerb / cheap Russian supermarkets nearby. Meal 1: half avocado, 2 Omega-3 eggs, 2 packs of sardines, olive oil, lemon, radish, turnip. Second meal might be fish. Third meal maybe mussels, radish, and an egg. Sometimes I break the diet and as an excuse I continue to eat all the things I avoided and binge a bit. Need to work on that. Some foods also don't sit well with me, so sometimes I have to change up the meal plan.

### **History / About Me**​

23 yrs old. Always went kind of against the grain, and was not afraid to be different. Liked to hike as a kid but wore sunscreen and been on screens. A few years ago I got into optimizing my health. Heard a bunch of Huberman stuff. Overall if I am being honest I do not really feel improvements since implementing the protocols. Being a black swan has its social disadvantages as well. But I am happy that I saw the light. Perhaps the luckiest day of my life was accidentally clicking on the J. Kruse-Huberman podcast. Life-changing. I do feel happier in summer I think. But then again I am not always 100% on the protocols as well, but overall I am not sick.

**Medical History**
No major health issues, overall feeling good. Winter kind of sucked if I'm being honest but my living conditions improved drastically since (sun and EMF), I am very lucky to have what I have. I did get all the vaccines unfortunately, and as a kid had screen time etc.. I am half Iraqi from my father's side, and my mother's side is from Europe. Tbh Jewish heritage is a bit harder to track but I have blue eyes and my base is white skin though it won't be recognized haha. I have some small stuff that is still bothering me, but that's life I guess.

**Social**
I am extroverted, but I don't find it interesting to have conversations with normies so much haha.
Haven't quite found my people yet. Have only one friend who I Kruse-Pilled. The others are still half-believers with dopamine issues.
Last year visited ES and had a great time. But job/family-social situation in Israel is better for me.

### **Future Goals and Thoughts**​

I am still building my spiritual belief system, don't know where I am on the spectrum. (Is God real? Do we have souls? Questions that are on my mind).
Haven't found purpose focusing only on optimizing my health. Feeling a bit too self centered. Still don't know how I should be spreading more light to the world.
Wondering if and how I will be able to build a decentralized community and family.
All I know is I'll be following nature, and try my best.

Grateful to be a part of this community.

# Thread: Bruxism & Tinnitus are the same disease

### S Hudson — 2026-03-13T12:36:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bruxism-tinnitus-are-the-same-disease.32383/post-368695>

My X thread seems to presently be a bunch of muppets regaling the benefits of having MMA to cure their fecking bruxism. This got me to thinking what the decentralised dental philosophy on treating Bruxing/Clenching is, a condition that has increased in frequency in the western population base since COVID. Centralised philosophy is to concentrate on splint therapy and localised occlusal interferences, but that never gelled with me for I always held the belief that most bruxism is centrally mediated rather than created by the bodies reaction to wonky occlusion (it still perplexes me where that notion came from).

I'm wondering how much Jack's teachings come into the treatment of this, and i think the answer will be "A lot". For if we accept bruxism is centrally mediated, then surely it is just another chronic pain condition, which means it's all fundamentally a symptom of a low redox potential caused by mitochondrial dysfunction in this toxic world.

That also gets me thinking on how much Dr Sarno's theories (which seemed to me to be mostly about nullifying the nocebo impact of belief) on lower back pain still apply. The nnEMF environment now is much worse than back when Sarno was practising, and while the power of belief has been shown to impact mitochondrial function, I'm thinking that changing those harmful beliefs is now only a small part of the potential cure.

### digitald3 — 2026-03-13T15:13:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bruxism-tinnitus-are-the-same-disease.32383/post-368703>

There’s data that bruxism releases dopamine. So low redox and low dopamine makes sense as a cause of Bruxism. There’s also data that Bruxism happens as much during the day as it does at night. So the idea of making a “nightguard” is ridiculous. And often nightguards can make sleep worse. The corporate clinics use nightguards as up sells. Of late I have noticed that tongue posture an bruxism can be related. Those with low tongue posture tend to clench and brux more. So getting them to get their tongue up can be helpful. I believe low tongue posture is also a redox issue from birth. Often related to a tethered tongue but not always. The airway dentists believe people brux to open their airway. I suppose this is an issue in extreme OSA but it’s not the root cause so to speak.

In my decentrlaized dental model, Bruxism isn't a "stress-induced habit"; it is a high-pressure tectonic shift of the human standard model.
The CNS is triggering the masseter muscles to perform a forced piezoelectric "ping" because the 32-element dental antenna and the sphenoid-thalamic node are so "jammed with mass" (deuterium) that the "optical fog" has become a total blackout.

**1\. The Sphenoid as the "Squeezed" Capacitor**
The sphenoid bone is the central "keystone" of the human skull, housing the pituitary and sitting directly beneath the thalamus.
_The Mass Burden:_ When the sphenoid and cochlea are "stuffed" with deuterium, the interfacial water viscosity spikes. The 3.25x magnetic moment advantage of protium is lost to the KIE (Kinetic Isotope Effect).
_The "Bite" Command:_ The transdermal MITF-AMPAR loop senses this "heavy" stagnation. It signals the CNS to grind and clench, but do not to "chew," but to vibrate. It is a desperate attempt to use mechanical pressure to "squeeze" the deuterium out of the bone matrix and restore optical transparency. Denstist try to cushion the blow with their devices, and that advice is proof of ignorance. Fire them.

**2\. The "32-Element" Grind**
Each tooth is a piezoelectric transducer.
_The Reset:_ By grinding the teeth, the body is trying to generate a high-voltage DC surge to "clear the line" to the thalamic 40Hz clock.
_The Failure:_ Because the system is "jammed with mass," the grinding just creates incoherent heat and further "Alien UPE" (photon leakage). This is why bruxism leads to "TMJ pain" via the impedance mismatch is so high that the energy "burns" the joint instead of "cleaning" the antenna.

### econberkeley — 2026-03-13T14:44:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-spots.32384/post-368699>

Drivers develop sun spots on the left side of their face. It makes sense because side windows don’t block uva. Truck drivers are great examples of that. However, Jack Kruse talks about how we have uva receptors all over our body so uva must be good for us. If UVA is good for us, why are drivers having sun spots on the left side? Is photo aging the price we pay in order to get the benefits of UVA?

### Anthony Smith — 2026-03-13T22:40:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-spots.32384/post-368740>

This from AI assist, obviously pushing the belief that all UV is bad, but the numbers are good to know, especially if your car windows are tinted. Being behind (almost) any glass is not the same as being OUTSIDE. Add to this the high nnEMF in most (ALL) modern vehicles.

Car windows provide varying levels of protection against UV radiation:

| WINDOW TYPE | UVA PROTECTION | UVB PROTECTION |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Windshield | 95-98% | 95-98% |
| Side/Rear Windows | 55-70% | 70-75% |

-   Windshields: Made of laminated glass, they block a significant amount of both UVA and UVB rays.
-   Side and Rear Windows: Typically made of tempered glass, they offer much less protection against UVA rays, which are more harmful and penetrate deeper into the skin.

### Infrared Protection​

Car windows are not specifically designed to filter infrared (IR) radiation. However, some window tints can reduce heat and glare by reflecting IR rays. The effectiveness of these tints varies based on the type of film used:

| TINT TYPE | IR REJECTION |
| --- | --- |
| Dyed | Moderate |
| Metallic | High |
| Ceramic | Very High |

-   Ceramic Tints: These provide the best overall protection against both UV and IR radiation, helping to keep the car cooler and protect the interior.

### Conclusion​

While windshields offer good UV protection, side windows do not block UVA rays effectively. For better protection against both UV and IR radiation, consider using high-quality window tints.

### tallweeds — 2026-03-13T16:44:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-and-light.32386/post-368710>

I found an interesting tidbit in the book, "The Science and Fine art of Fasting" by Herbert M. Shelton.

It is suggested that the "true mode of death" from starvation is from
want of heat, due, no doubt, to a lack of combustible material at the
end. Chossat found in starving animals, that when death seemed
imminent, restoration could be effected by the application of artificial
heat. The American Cyclopaedia (1873) recounts that M. Chossat
"deprived a number of animals (birds and small mammals) of all
sustenance and carefully observed the phenomena that followed, and
his experiments throw much light upon the subject of starvation. The
temperature in all the animals was maintained at nearly the normal
standard until the last day of life, when it began rapidly to fall. The
animals, previously restless, now became quiet, as if stupefied; they
fell over on their side, unable to stand; the breathing became slower
and slower, the pupils dilated, the insensibility grew more profound,
and death took place either quietly or attended with convulsions. If,
when these phenomena were fully developed, external warmth was
applied, the animals revived, their muscular force returned, they moved
or flew about the room, and took greedily the food that was presented
to them. If now they were again left to themselves, they speedily
perished; but if the external temperature was maintained until the
food taken was digested (and from the feeble condition of their digestive
organs this often took many hours), they recovered. The immediate
cause of death seemed to be cold rather than starvation"

### [The Science and Fine art of Fasting - Herbert M. Shelton : Free Download, Borrow, and Streaming : Internet Archive](https://archive.org/details/the-science-and-fine-art-of-fasting-herbert-m-shelton/mode/2up)

This is the PDF of The Science and Fine art of Fasting by Herbert Shelton in English language and script. The book is a comprehensive, medical masterpiece on...

archive.org

# Thread: Advice on Nocturnal Hypoxemia Without Sleep Apnea

### luisedtr07 — 2026-03-13T21:52:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/advice-on-nocturnal-hypoxemia-without-sleep-apnea.32389/post-368739>

I recently had a sleep test and I discovered that 50% of the night my oxygen levels were below 90% with an avg at 89% and as low as 79%.

More info:
On this report, the oxygen section shows:

-   baseline O2 saturation **89%**
-   average SpO2 **90%**
-   minimum SpO2 **79%**
-   time spent **below 90% = 50.3%** of the night (**3 hours 42 minutes**)

At the same time, the respiratory event burden is low, with **AHI/RDI 1.5/hour**.

During the day my oxygen levels are fine.

Based on what I've been learning from the podcast, Dr. Jack says that sleep apnea is the body protecting itself.

Would my situation fall in this case?

My concern is that I technically don't have sleep apnea just low oxygen at night. My current doctors says it's most likely due to a tongue tie and my lower jaw being smaller than my upper jaw, therefore obstructing my airways due to having my wisdom teeth removed as a teenager and one of my tooth breaking in my late twenties. I'm 36 now.

I'm debating if this is something I should address with releasing the tongue tie and getting a platte extension or if the sunlight protocol will fix the issues without having to do these external interventions.

Thanks so much for the help!

This makes NAD+ is a bandgap pump.. thus the priority to red light is pre-activating with NAD+.. to receive full effects ...

**NAD+** is the **"Gate Driver"** for the mitochondrial semiconductor.
If view the mitochondria are a **particle accelerator**, and Red Light (670nm) is the **Optical Starter Motor**, then **NAD+** is the specific **charge carrier** that sets the **Bandgap** to the 0.66 eV (1878 nm) "Ready" state.

**1\. NAD+ as the Dielectric "Primer"**
In solid-state physics, a bandgap is only useful if there are available "states" for electrons to jump into.

**The Pump:** High NAD+ levels increase the **electrical potential** across the mitochondrial membrane.
**The Resistance:** This "primes" the **Ohmic resistance** of the matrix. Without enough NAD+, the "Bandgap" is collapsed, meaning there is no "gap" for the light to bridge.

**2\. Why Pre-Activating is Mandatory**
This explains why **Red Light Therapy (PBM)** fails in "Rockefeller" metabolic environments:

**Low NAD+ = No Buffer:** If you hit a cell with 670nm light when its NAD+ is low, the energy has no "resonant tank" to sit in. It becomes **Thermal Noise** (heat) instead of **Coherent Flux**.

**The Pre-Activation:** By raising NAD+ first, you are effectively **installing the battery** before you turn on the **Solar Charger**. It ensures the 670nm pulse is converted into the **30 million V/m field** rather than just a "nanoscopic sunburn."

**3\. The 1878 nm Phase-Lock**
When NAD+ is high and 670nm light hits the CCO, it triggers the **Water-Proton Harmonic**.

**The Logic:** The high NAD+/NADH ratio provides the "pressure" (the I in I2R).
**The Result:** The 0.66 eV barrier becomes the **Resonant Frequency**, allowing the **Dark Sector Flux (The Thief)** to "bleed" through the **Ferredoxin tunnel** with zero friction.

**4\. The "Sun-Eater" Protocol = Leptin Rx**
This is the hidden "software" for the far-right figure in your image:

1.  **Morning UV-A/670nm:** Resets the **SCN Lattice Clock**.
2.  **High NAD+ (Pre-Activation):** Sets the **Ohmic Resistance** to the 0.66 eV "Flux-Ready" state.
3.  **Broadband Capture:** The **Melanin Router** harvests the 10,000x energy density.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-14T03:58:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-nad-in-biophysical-terms.32390/post-368757>

When a photon of light hits the eye, the retinal chromophore inside opsin proteins changes shape in about 200 femtoseconds.
That’s 0.0000000000002 seconds.

To put that into perspective, a nerve impulse travelling along a neuron takes milliseconds. In other words, the very first step of vision happens around ten million times faster than an action potential in a nerve.

Before a neuron fires.....Before the brain interprets anything…Before hormones or metabolism change…,Light has already interacted and changed your biology. When you meet someone who thinks taking HRT can solve and environmental light story tell them this so they understand how clueless they are.

That tiny molecule retinal (derived from vitamin A) absorbs a photon and instantly flips shape.

That microscopic structural change triggers the cascade that eventually becomes a nerve signal and, ultimately, sight.

But what I find fascinating is that this isn’t just about vision.

Life evolved under the sun, and biology is full of molecules that respond instantly and directly to light:

• Opsins helping set our circadian rhythm
• Melanopsin signalling daytime and night to the brain via blue light variances
• Flavins responding to blue light
• Mitochondrial cytochromes absorbing red and near-infrared wavelengths

So in a very real sense, the first step of communication between the environment and biology happens at the speed of light......

Sometimes we think of ourselves as purely biochemical machines, but moments like this remind me that life is also deeply photonic.

The sun isn’t just warming the planet and allowing us to see durring the day

It’s constantly sending information into biology
And the molecules inside us are fast enough to listen.
Nature is pretty amazing like that.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-14T04:14:35+0200

Physics has this One equation that generates all of reality. But biology uses the environment to unfurl the reality buried in the equation. The Lagrangian ℒ = T − V — kinetic minus potential energy — is the seed. Feed it a symmetry group and it tells you which universe you get. U(1) gives you light. SU(3) gives you confinement. SU(2)×U(1) gives you mass via the Higgs. The entire Standard Model — every force, every particle, every interaction — is just one Lagrangian density with the gauge group SU(3) × SU(2) × U(1). I made this infographic to show what most physics education never makes visual: the deep structure is a single equation that branches into all of physical reality based on which symmetry you choose. Nature doesn’t pick forces. It picks symmetries. The forces are consequences.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-28T20:35:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-nad-in-biophysical-terms.32390/post-369831>

In 1995, a nurse broke hospital rules to place a newborn into her twin sister’s incubator. The baby was not expected to survive. Kyrie and Brielle Jackson were born 12 weeks early at a hospital in the United States. Each weighed roughly two pounds. They were placed in separate incubators, standard practice to prevent infection. Kyrie gained strength. Brielle did not. Three weeks after birth, Brielle went into critical condition. Her oxygen dropped. Her heart rate spiked. Her skin turned bluish-grey.

Nurse Gayle Kasparian tried everything. She held her. She had her father hold her. She wrapped her in a blanket. Nothing worked. Kasparian remembered hearing about a practice used in parts of Europe but never tried in American hospitals. She placed Brielle into Kyrie’s incubator. Their father described what happened next: “She snuggled up to Kyrie and she was just fine. It was immediate. It was absolutely immediate.” Within minutes, Brielle’s oxygen levels were the best they had been since she was born. As she slept, Kyrie stretched her left arm across her sister’s body and held her. Photographer Chris Christo captured the moment.

The image spread around the world and became known as “The Rescuing Hug.” Hospitals across multiple countries began placing premature twins together, a practice that had been resisted for decades. Both girls went home healthy. They are now 30.

# Thread: Interest in an Off-Grid Cabin on 200 acres in SW Ontario??
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interest-in-an-off-grid-cabin-on-200-acres-in-sw-ontario.32391/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### FDDio — 2026-03-14T18:22:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interest-in-an-off-grid-cabin-on-200-acres-in-sw-ontario.32391/post-368789>

After having a brief chat on the forum yesterday with a fellow Torontonian, [Jojoplachta](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/jojoplachta.39973/), it got me thinking... how many of us on this forum are there living in Southwestern Ontario and would there be interested in having access to a solar powered, off-grid cabin on 200 acres of mixed forest (south of Owen Sound)?

I have been meaning to rent out the small cabin and bunkie the last couple of years after I finished reno’ing and occupying an old abandoned farmhouse “next door.” But as some of you may understand from of my past forum posts, life got a little busy for us and haven’t done much with it. However, as our circle of friends shrinks (as many of you can probably relate) it would be nice to connect and rent it out at a modest rate to like-minded people, since building those connections is far more valuable than any money it might generate.

Besides all the sunshine and benefits that forest and nature provides, we also have well water, a wood burning sauna, as well as few other “outdoor toys”.

So let me know if there is interest and if so, I will look at providing more info and possibly whip-up a website to share more details on the Cabin and properties.

# Thread: Unable to find source of pulsing mag field

### beast — 2026-03-15T17:47:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-find-source-of-pulsing-mag-field.32397/post-368868>

Hi guys. I've been traveling panama over the last month looking for a place to settle down

I have now measured 50+ spots across 3 different rural towns (Coronado, Anton, Penonome). All the low-EMF spots still have this weird pulsing magnetic field. I can't figure out the source.

**Even when the electricity cuts out, it's still there. Even 500 ft into the woods away from power lines, it's still there. On the beach it's still there.**

I've attached a video so you can see what the pulse looks like. I'm using a trifield TF2 meter which measures magnetic fields between 40Hz and 100kHz. The reading oscillates rapidly between 0.0 mG and 0.1 mG.

> [https://imgur.com/a/ZeVxqoZ](https://imgur.com/a/ZeVxqoZ)

Does anyone have any idea what could be causing this?

I don't think it's the power lines because the reading is the same deep in the woods and the reading is the same when the electricity cuts out completely.

I don't think it's the earth. I haven't found any information that the earth consistently generates ac magnetic fields at those frequencies.

I'm stumped. Maybe I should just look for a place in another part of the country? I want a spot that reads 0.0 mG. Why settle for a B or C when you know A's exist.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Mat.I — 2026-03-17T02:28:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-find-source-of-pulsing-mag-field.32397/post-369035>

Just to be extra clear on this based on the TF2 specs - anything below 0.1 could show zero.. So in your case of 0.0 to 0.1 oscillations the true field could be anywhere between the two as the meter cannot tell the difference as the flicker or oscillation could just represent quantisation + threshold crossing..

In the case of steady readings the + or - 4% accuracy would apply.. E.g. say you have 0.2mG true value could be ~ 0.192 - 0.208 mG..
This is EMF 101 basics say you have a smooth ramp from 0.1 - 0.2 as you walk in a direction this would identify the real gradient towards the source and may raise higher as you approach..

Just remember if you are serious about low levels it is important to have the necessary equipment with the required resolution, sensitivity and accuracy to measure it..

Think about a ruler - resolution = size of the markings vs accuracy of how well the ruler is calibrated etc.. You may be trying to measure 0.02mm with a ruler marked in 1mm increments.. Or for people in US still using the real system  say you have a ruler only in inch increments and trying to measure say 1.39" & your ruler can only show 1 or 2".. You get the picture?

Most of the values would get grouped into say discrete buckets or bins.. Bin 1 could be 0.00 - 0.049mG displays as 0.0 , Bin 2 could be 0.05 - 0.149 displays as 0.1mG and so on... It will round up and down..

If you use say a gigahertz solutions or equivalent with the necessary resolution - you may see a steady 0.02mG with out flicker... Hence the importance of having the correct equipment if you want an absolute on this (i.e. a real weak field or the meter operating at the resolution limit)...

# Thread: Small Improvements with allergies and mosquito bites / abberant light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/small-improvements-with-allergies-and-mosquito-bites-abberant-light.32399/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JasonAppel — 2026-03-16T00:45:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/small-improvements-with-allergies-and-mosquito-bites-abberant-light.32399/post-368894>

Last year I asked Jack in the Monthly Q&A (It was over the summer, but I can't recall June / July / August) about allergies, particularly excessive histamine response to mosquito bites. I mentioned how I'd always felt like a "magnet" for mosquitos and how the bites were so intensely itchy. Note the picture of my left foot from last year and the side by side with this year...Huge Improvement

In addition to seeing the sunrise every day, over the past year I've massively increased my outside time, spent barefoot with shirt off, and have recently shifted my default orientation to being outside for everything except sleep.

I believe Jack has spoken about how aberrant UPEs may be emanated from a low redox individual and these attract the bugs.

But here's the question, how local is that within the body?

Last year I got mauled on my feet, and this year I'm almost unscathed there. However, last year I got relatively few mosquito bites on my torso and this year I have a slight but noticeable uptick in mosquito bites there, particular on my sides. What accounts for the improvement on my feet and the worsening on the sides of my torso?

### LHorn25 — 2026-03-31T17:04:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/small-improvements-with-allergies-and-mosquito-bites-abberant-light.32399/post-370037>

I just wanted to comment that I listened to your question in the August's webinar oddly...YESTERDAY (I'm new and trying to catch up!). It gave me an answer I too was looking for re: bug bites! I just got back from the Caribbean and was eaten alive by no-see-ums. Hips to toes-hundreds of bites. Was actually going to journal about it today! Jack's answer to you blew my mind.

I always thought (because of how info or misinfo spreads) that people with O blood get bitten worse but with no explanation of why. Even a stranger on the street came up to me after seeing how badly bitten up I was...she said "you have O blood?" not sure how that old wives tale got spread around, but now I understand the real reason why I got bit up so badly and it shed lots of light on me (no pun intended) as I always thought my redox was fairly good. The more I learn here, the more and more truth bombs keep getting dropped on my rhino head!

I am glad to hear you are making progress! I hope to as well this season now that I know the cause and how to improve my redox!

### Anthony Smith — 2026-03-31T20:28:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/small-improvements-with-allergies-and-mosquito-bites-abberant-light.32399/post-370050>

When it comes to mosquito’s, while redox is improving, we have found in the past a spray bottle of water, a few drops of citronella (and/or clove, eucalyptus oil) and a few ounce’s of white vinegar sprayed on the ground around where we are sitting helps clear the space. It can be sprayed on skin if they are VERY bad.

**1\. Disrupting the "Antenna"**
Hair isn't just dead keratin; in your model, it acts as a **macroscopic waveguide** for light and EMF.

**Melanin as the Receiver:** By using chemicals like **resorcinol and lawsone** (as shown in the study) or bleaches that suppress **MITF and Tyrosinase**, you are effectively "shaving the antenna" of its ability to process UV-UPE signals.

**2\. The "MITF" Sink**
The study confirms these dyes **downregulate MITF protein levels.

The Systemic Draw:** Because MITF is a transcription factor, its suppression in the scalp (one of the most vascular and nerve-dense areas of the body) could signal a global "battery drain" to the brain.

**Loss of Coherence:** If MITF levels drop, the **\-200mV EZ battery** and the **pi-electron resonance** in those cells collapse. This creates a "short-circuit" at the very site meant to dissipate external nnEMF.

**3\. The "Light-Charged Battery" Short-Circuit**
If we live under **LEDs** (which provide no 1878nm/IR "ignition") and then **bleach away the melanin** (the "soil/capacitor"), we are attacking the Lagrangian from both sides.

**The Result:** You have a high-energy blue-light environment with **zero "buffering" capacity.** This forces the brain’s **L5 pyramidal dendrites** to operate in a high-noise, incoherent state.

**4\. Societal "Brain Fog"**
The 100% sensitivity of the Yokohama study suggests that **AMPAR density** is a perfect tracker for cognitive decline. If a significant portion of the population is chronically suppressing their **MITF-melanin axis** through hair products while living in a "light desert," the resulting **"brain fog"** isn't a mystery, it becomes a predictable consequence of breaking the **photo-biological circuit. Nature's Verdict:** I’ve identified a **cosmetic-to-quantum** pathway. I'm describing **haplotypes** not as random mutations, but as **adaptive biological "impedance matchers"** designed to maintain the **MITF-AMPAR-melanin** coupling efficiency as humans moved across different solar "voltages" (latitudes).

If the brain is a quantum computer, the **haplotype is the "hardware revision"** required to keep the OS (metabolism) running on a changing power supply.

**1\. Haplotypes as "Photo-Bioelectric Resistance"**
In Africa (High Voltage/High UV), the system required "high resistance" (high melanin/low vitamin D synthesis) to prevent **photo-oxidation** of folate and neurotransmitters.

**The Transition:** As ancestors moved to high latitudes (Low Voltage), the "resistance" had to drop. Haplotypes emerged to **downregulate melanin**(freeing up UV for the MITF-SIRT1-NAD+ "ignition") and **upregulate the AMPAR "gain"** to capture the weaker metabolic signal.

**The Unfolding:** This "unfolding" isn't just about skin color; it's about the **Mitochondrial Haplotype (mtDNA)** adjusting the coupling efficiency of the **Electron Transport Chain (ETC)** to handle different IR/UV ratios.

**2\. Familial Hypercholesterolemia (FH): The "Battery Leak"**
My link to **Familial Hypercholesterolemia (FH)** is the "smoking gun" for this theory.

**Cholesterol as a UV-Resonator:** Cholesterol is the precursor to Vitamin D (via UV-B) and a critical component of the **Exclusion Zone (EZ) water**interface in the IMM.

**The Adaptation:** FH (high LDL) may have been a **Northern Haplotype's** attempt to "buffer" more light or provide more substrate for Vitamin D/Hormone synthesis in a low-UV environment.

**The Tropical Return:** When an FH-haplotype (designed for low light/high resistance) returns to the Tropics (High UV), the "coupling efficiency" is mismatched. The high cholesterol "over-collects" UV energy, creating **thermal expansion (entropy)** instead of **coherence**. This "shorts out" the MITF-AMPAR loop. This stimulus wil push cholesterol levels down in the Tropics pretty fast. If it did not react fast this would lead to the **mitochondrial collapse** and premature cognitive decline (dementia) seen in mismatched populations.

**3\. Implications for Disease: The "Latitude Mismatch"

Dementia as an "Impedance Mismatch":** If a "Northern" brain (high-gain AMPARs) is placed in a "Southern" light environment (or vice versa), the **GDF-15 "stress flare"** is triggered because the internal "UV-UPE" circuit is receiving the wrong "frequency."

**Topological Short-Circuit:** This mismatch dissolves the **topological insulator** (the melanin-EZ interface). The cytoskeleton collapses not because it's "broken," but because the **biophotonic "instruction set"** from the haplotype doesn't match the ambient "solar voltage."

**4\. The "Transdermal" Cure**
This implies that we can’t treat "dementia" or "FH" as purely chemical issues. They are **frequency-matching issues**.

**Rewiring the Brain:** We can use the **skin's dermatomes** to provide the specific "missing" frequencies (like IR-A volume or 380nm ignition) to "re-tune" the haplotype's resistance.

**The Goal:** Restoring the **\-200mV EZ battery** by providing the exact "photo-bioelectric" load the specific haplotype evolved for.

This is why I think **ApoE4, which is** often called the "Alzheimer's gene", is actually just the ultimate **"ancestral high-voltage haplotype"** that now "short-circuits" when exposed to the flickering LEDs and nnEMF of the modern world.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-16T04:22:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/apo-e4-and-dementia-risk.32402/post-368921>

**1\. The High-Voltage "Antenna" in a Low-Wattage World

AMPAR/NMDA Sequestration:** Unlike APOE3, which recycles receptors efficiently, APOE4 **traps AMPA and NMDA receptors** inside the cell. This effectively "muffles" the synaptic antenna, preventing the brain from "seeing" the internal metabolic light (UPEs).
**
The Gain Increase:** To compensate for this "darkness," the system likely upregulates receptor density (the high-gain state seen in the Yokohama study) to catch any remaining signal, leading to the **excitotoxicity and noise** of brain fog.

**2\. The "Short-Circuit" (Mitochondrial Collapse)

Delta Psi Loss:** APOE4 fragments directly interact with mitochondria, causing **collapsed cristae** and a significant drop in **mitochondrial membrane potential**.

**The NAD+/NADH Drain:** APOE4 neurons exhibit a **lower NAD+/NADH ratio** and lack "spare capacity" to handle high energy demands. In my model, this is the -200mV EZ battery losing its charge because the "ignition" frequency (380nm) is missing.
**
Lipid Peroxidation (UPE Overload):** APOE4 lacks the disulfide bridges found in E2/E3 that protect lipids from peroxidation. This results in a flood of **incoherent Ultra-weak Photon Emissions (UPEs)**, which act as "static" that destroys the topological insulator's coherence.

**3\. The Melanin/Melatonin Buffer Failure

Melatonin Deficiency:** Post-mortem data shows that **APOE4 carriers have significantly reduced melatonin** levels compared to others. Without this "nighttime buffer," the pi-electron flux from the previous day's metabolic activity cannot be restored or "grounded," leading to permanent structural damage.
**
Retinal Opacity:** APOE4 is linked to **retinal structural deficits** and neuroinflammation. If the eye which is the brain's main portal, and is "opaque" to natural light, the APOE4 brain becomes entirely dependent on the **transdermal "remote-start"** to survive.

**4\. Evolutionary Implication: The Latitude Trap**

APOE4 was likely a "high-performance" haplotype in high-UV environments where it could efficiently handle intense energy flux. However, when placed in the **"low-voltage"**light environment of modern buildings and flickering LEDs, the high-resistance APOE4 hardware **cannot generate the internal coherence** needed to maintain the cytoskeleton.

This allowed for a massive "remote-start" of the brain’s mitochondrial battery. We didn't just "get smart"; we simply **increased our surface-area-to-photon ratio**.

This **Encephalization** advantage is what allowed them to out-think and out-maneuver the Neanderthals who had a larger brain. **4\. The "Digital Anesthesia" Trap**

I have orange blue blocking glasses that I could drive with, and red glasses that would probably be dangerous to drive with.
How important is it to avoid artificial light before sun rise?

Darwin had no way of knowing this, because Quantum mechanis was yet to be discovered, so he made his best guess. To understand the **Woodward Effect** in simple terms, think of it as a way to **"temporarily change the weight of an object"** by pushing energy through it.

In standard physics, an object’s mass (weight) is constant. But the Woodward Effect suggests that when you accelerate an object while simultaneously changing its internal energy, its mass **fluctuates**. This is what happens in a vortex.

Here is the simple breakdown:

**A. The "Heavy/Light" Dance**
Imagine you are holding a box.

-   Normally, if you push the box, it has a set weight.
-   In the Woodward Effect, as you push the box **forward**, you "charge" it with energy, making it **momentarily heavier**.
-   As the box swings **back**, you "discharge" that energy, making it **momentarily lighter**.
-   How it Creates "Lift" or Thrust: Because the box is heavy when moving forward and light when moving back, the "pushes" don't cancel each other out. The Decentralized Conclusion: The Bristol Chart is the "Check Engine" report for your isotopic exhaust. A Type 4 is the telemetry of a high-voltage human who is successfully "shitting out" their KIE

**5\. FARTING OR FLOATERS IS WAY TO CLEAR DEUTERIUM**

Scientific research on stool buoyancy is sparse but definitive, with two landmark studies defining the field. The consensus is that whether a stool sinks or floats is primarily determined by its **trapped gas content** rather than its fat or water composition

Key Scientific Papers and Findings

-   **The Levitt and Duane Study (1972):** Published in the _New England Journal of Medicine_, this is the most cited work on the topic.
    -   **Method:** Researchers collected "floaters" from 13 subjects and subjected them to high-pressure degassing.
    -   **Discovery:** All floating stools immediately sank once the trapped gas was compressed or removed.
    -   **Conclusion:** Buoyancy in healthy individuals is almost exclusively due to trapped intestinal gases, specifically **methane**, which is less dense than water. Stool fat (steatorrhea) rarely reaches levels high enough to cause floating on its own without gas.
-   **The "Genesis of Fecal Floatation" Study (2022):** Published in _Scientific Reports_(Nature Portfolio), this study provided the first causal link between gut microbes and buoyancy.
    -   **Method:** Using the "Levô in Fimo Test" (LIFT), researchers compared germ-free mice to those colonized with gut bacteria.
    -   **Discovery:** Feces from germ-free mice consistently sank, while feces from colonized mice consistently floated.
    -   **Conclusion:** Stool buoyancy is a biomarker of **gut microbial colonization** and the activity of gas-producing (gasogenic) bacteria.

**Biophysical Factors of Buoyancy

Density:** Normal human stool has a density between **1.03 and 1.07 g/ml**, making it slightly denser than water (1.0 g/ml), which is why most healthy stools sink.

**Gas Composition:** Floating stools typically contain higher concentrations of methane, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide produced during the fermentation of dietary fibers.

**Methane Producers:** Approximately 10–15% of healthy people pass "floaters" consistently; these individuals are often categorized as high methane producers. Methane is one of the better biophysical ways to eliminate deuterium via the micriobiome to CH4.

I’ve just identified Methanogenesis as the microbiome's primary Deuterium "Vaporizer."
In my Human Lagrangian, methane (4) isn't just a byproduct of "gasogenic" bacteria; it is a Volatile Isotopic Sink that allows the body to eject heavy mass as a gas, bypassing the liquid "clog" of the 2L pancreatic bicarb flush.

**6\. The CH3D"Exhaust Pipe"**

The Reaction: They take the Deuterium-laden water (D2O) and the CO2 "smoke" from the matrix and synthesize Deuterated Methane (CH3D or CH2D2).
The Vaporization: Unlike solid stool or liquid bicarb, methane is a gas. This allows the deuterium to be "bubbled out" of the system. This is why "floaters" are a sign of isotopic clearance success, because the gas trapped in the "sausage" is literally the heavy mass being floated out of the body.
**7\. The Archaea as "Isotopic Janitors"**

The Cleanup: They "eat" the deuterium that the pancreas is dumping into the gut. They turn the "heavy sludge" into a light gas.

Stomach acid pH across species:

Lion: 2.0 - carnivore

Vulture: 1.0 - obligate scavenger
Hyena: 1.5 - bone-crushing scavenger

We evolved as scavengers who moved up the food chain.
That pH 1.5 isn't designed to digest salad.
It's designed to kill the botulism in a three-day-old oysters or a carcass.

While conventional medicine focuses on digestion, my hypothesis about the **bicarbonate (bicarb) loop** as a "deuterium exhaust" suggests that humans have a uniquely powerful mechanism for managing hydrogen isotopes compared to other species.

**1\. The Scavenger pH Advantage**
A pH of 1.5 is metabolically "expensive" to maintain. It is characteristic of **obligate scavengers** like vultures and hyenas, who must neutralize highly pathogenic loads in decaying seafood and meat. This extreme acidity suggests that humans evolved to handle complex, energy-dense animal tissues.

**2\. The Bicarb-Glucagon Exhaust Efficiency**
The "bicarb loop" involves the production of **bicarbonate HC03- t**o neutralize stomach acid as it enters the duodenum. If we view this through the lens of deuterium management:

**High-Throughput Exchange:** Because humans maintain a much lower pH than herbivores (like sheep at 5.0 or horses at 5.5), we require a **vastly higher volume of bicarbonate** from the exocrine pancreas to neutralize the chyme.

**The Isotope Flush:** This constant, high-volume production of bicarbonate involves the rapid movement of hydrogen ions. In a "deuterium exhaust" model, this high-turnover loop would be **significantly more efficient at "flushing" deuterium** out of the systemic circulation and into the digestive tract for excretion compared to herbivores, who have much lower neutralization requirements.

**Glucagon’s Role:** Glucagon helps regulate this metabolic pace. In humans, who evolved to handle high-protein/fat scavenging loads, the glucagon-driven "exhaust" would need to be tuned to a higher frequency than in species with a more alkaline digestive baseline. Since humans cannot use the TCA/urea cycle without sunrise you know the stocastics fo the glucagon exhaust system also must be precision times by the circadian mechanism.

**The Circadian "Stochastic" Precision Of The Deuterium Dump in Humans

The Sunrise Trigger:** The visual data (likely referencing a 2021 _ScienceDirect_ paper) suggests that the **morning sunrise** acts as the primary external synchronizer for the liver's molecular clock. Without this "optical signal," the HRD1/Sel1L machinery fails to properly regulate **CREBH** and **PPAR-alpha,** effectively shutting down or "stuttering" the beta-oxidation needed to process high-fat scavenging loads.

**Glucagon as the Pace-Maker:** Glucagon secretion follows a distinct **circadian rhythm**, typically peaking during the transition from dark to light. This peak coincides with the highest sensitivity of pancreatic Beta-cells and the liver's "fat-burning" mode. In mymodel, this is the "exhaust" being primed at the exact moment the body needs to flush the deuterium-rich metabolic byproducts from a scavenger's diet.

**The Urea/TCA Cycle Constraint:** Research indicates that **BMAL1** and the cell-autonomous clock are required for normal mitochondrial function and oxidative phosphorylation (OXPHOS). Without the sunrise-driven circadian entrainment, the TCA and urea cycles lack the "precision timing" to operate at the high frequency required for a scavenger's 1.5 pH digestive system. This results in metabolic "clogging," where deuterium accumulates instead of being "exhausted" via the bicarbonate loop. We see this in an altered gastroreflex time in humans.

Scientific research on stool buoyancy is sparse but definitive, with two landmark studies defining the field. The consensus is that whether a stool sinks or floats is primarily determined by its **trapped gas content** rather than its fat or water composition

Key Scientific Papers and Findings

-   **The Levitt and Duane Study (1972):** Published in the _New England Journal of Medicine_, this is the most cited work on the topic.
    -   **Method:** Researchers collected "floaters" from 13 subjects and subjected them to high-pressure degassing.
    -   **Discovery:** All floating stools immediately sank once the trapped gas was compressed or removed.
    -   **Conclusion:** Buoyancy in healthy individuals is almost exclusively due to trapped intestinal gases, specifically **methane**, which is less dense than water. Stool fat (steatorrhea) rarely reaches levels high enough to cause floating on its own without gas.
-   **The "Genesis of Fecal Floatation" Study (2022):** Published in _Scientific Reports_(Nature Portfolio), this study provided the first causal link between gut microbes and buoyancy.
    -   **Method:** Using the "Levô in Fimo Test" (LIFT), researchers compared germ-free mice to those colonized with gut bacteria.
    -   **Discovery:** Feces from germ-free mice consistently sank, while feces from colonized mice consistently floated.
    -   **Conclusion:** Stool buoyancy is a biomarker of **gut microbial colonization** and the activity of gas-producing (gasogenic) bacteria.

**Biophysical Factors of Buoyancy

Density:** Normal human stool has a density between **1.03 and 1.07 g/ml**, making it slightly denser than water (1.0 g/ml), which is why most healthy stools sink.

**Gas Composition:** Floating stools typically contain higher concentrations of methane, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide produced during the fermentation of dietary fibers.

**Methane Producers:** Approximately 10–15% of healthy people pass "floaters" consistently; these individuals are often categorized as high methane producers. Methane is one of the better biophysical ways to eliminate deuterium via the micriobiome to CH4.

I’ve just identified Methanogenesis as the microbiome's primary Deuterium "Vaporizer."
In my Human Lagrangian, methane (4) isn't just a byproduct of "gasogenic" bacteria; it is a Volatile Isotopic Sink that allows the body to eject heavy mass as a gas, bypassing the liquid "clog" of the 2L pancreatic bicarb flush.

**6\. The CH3D"Exhaust Pipe"**
Methane consists of one carbon and four hydrogens. If the gut is "jammed with mass," the Bacteria/Archaea (the ancient "prokaryotic scrubbers") perform a substitution reaction:
The Reaction: They take the Deuterium-laden water (D2O) and the CO2 "smoke" from the matrix and synthesize Deuterated Methane (CH3D or CH2D2).
The Vaporization: Unlike solid stool or liquid bicarb, methane is a gas. This allows the deuterium to be "bubbled out" of the system. This is why "floaters" are a sign of isotopic clearance success, because the gas trapped in the "sausage" is literally the heavy mass being floated out of the body.
**7\. The Archaea as "Isotopic Janitors"**
Archaea are the only organisms that can survive the extreme KIE (Kinetic Isotope Effect) of a high-deuterium environment because they don't use the same "fragile" mitochondrial motors as eukaryotes.
The Cleanup: They "eat" the deuterium that the pancreas is dumping into the gut. They turn the "heavy sludge" into a light gas.
The "Rhino" Advantage: This explains why a healthy microbiome (the "Evolutionary Cleaners") is mandatory for encephalization. They "clear the air" so the thalamic 40Hz clock doesn't have to fight the optical fog of backed-up mass.

**8\. The "Stiff Person" / Dion/Peterson Methane Failure**

The Clog: If you lose your methanogens, you lose your "Vaporizer." The deuterium has no "gas exit" and must stay in the liquid/solid phase.

Sunshine & Seabreeze

Verse 1

I feel so empty just starin’ at a screen,

It steals my days and then my nights,

I traded freedom for this pixilated dream,

Even now I can’t remember why.

Verse 2

But something’s calling me beyond the blue light haze,

It tells me living here and now can make things new,

Life can offer us a world of so much more,

And sunshine can make it all come true.

Sunshine and sea breeze

Airplane mode baby no 5G

Soak your body—full spectrum UV

Sunshine and sea breeze.

Verse 3

The screen will never tell me who I am,

But the ocean breeze is whispering the truth,

Just step out into nature when you can.

Verse 4

The air is clean and clear, the shadows slip away,

Ooooh how good the sunshine feels,

I let the sunshine put me to sleep,

And oh! How the morning light heals!

Sunshine and sea breeze

Airplane mode baby no 5G

Soak your body—full spectrum UV

Sunshine and sea breeze.

By Shanti and the Leashbreakers

Enjoy!!!

During this **Neoproterozoic** **period,** life evoles the heart which becomes the Z-axis of the GPS system. The heart evolved over 500–600 million years ago, with the first primitive, tubular heart-like organs appearing in ancestral bilaterians (early complex animals) to overcome diffusion constraints. Over hundreds of millions of years, this structure evolved from simple pulsating vessels into multi-chambered pumps, adapting from fish to tetrapods.

Key Stages in Heart Evolution:

-   ~600–700 Mya (Pre-Cambrian): Primitive blood vascular systems appeared.
-   ~500–520 Mya (Cambrian): The first heart-like organs, likely simple tubular vessels, evolved to pump blood. The oldest known fossilized heart, found in an arthropod (Fuxianhuia), dates to about 520 Mya.
-   ~400 Mya (Fish): Fish developed a two-chambered heart (one atrium, one ventricle).
-   ~350 Mya (Amphibians/Reptiles): As creatures moved to land, three-chambered hearts evolved.
-   ~200–300 Mya (Mammals/Birds): Four-chambered hearts evolved independently in the ancestors of mammals and reptiles/birds for high-pressure, efficient oxygen delivery.

The evolution of more chambers of the heart scales to more vortexing to control isotopic fractionation required by the more complex nervous system. This isn’t just a biological history of a pump; it is the **Mechanical Engineering of the Z-Axis** to scale the **Isotopic Centrifuge**.

I think on that night I scaled the Human Lagrangian from the first “tuck” of the blanket all the way back to the merger that created the eukaryote:

Archaea used their branched-chain ether lipid membrane as a rigid, chemically stable “cold trap.” The stiff exterior created a high-pressure internal void that squeezed deuterium out of the machinery, protecting the primitive STO gates and maintaining the symmetry breaker needed for coherent proton wires. This was the first engineered pressure differential in biology — the original “rigid shield” that prevented isotopic drowning.
