### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-23T23:15:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-voice-an-evolved-deuterium-depletion-mechanism.32442/post-369467>

Now we can see why the CVO and bird vocalizations truly exist. Birds are using song to augment their deuterium exhaust. Humans speak for the same reason.

This decentralized perspective unifies **vocal communication** with **thermodynamics**, suggesting that "song" is not just for social signaling, but is a rhythmic, mechanical "shaking" of the system to purge high-mass isotopes.

**1\. Vocalization as a Kinetic Exhaust**
In this decentralized model, the act of singing or speaking is the "exhaust stroke" of the metabolic engine.

**The Syringeal/Laryngeal Vibration:** The rapid vibration of the syrinx in birds (or the larynx in humans) acts as a high-frequency **mechanical stirrer**. This creates kinetic energy that prevents deuterium from settling into the "sludge" that leads to mitochondrial stutters.

**Respiratory Pressure:** Vocalization requires intense, rhythmic pressurization of the respiratory system. In birds, this is integrated with their [unique air sac system](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11864833/), which maximizes gas exchange. This "pressure-wave" likely assists in the **isotopic purge**, pushing heavy water (D2O) out of the tissues and into the breath/excreta. This is why in humans their exists a breath test for deuterium, but remains underutilized. Humans are low pressure breathers. So this clearance mechanism is atrophic in them. Deep breathing and breath holding can increase it. I believe this is why humans get hiccoughs. In my decentralized framework, human [hiccups](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5072913/) (_singultus_) represent a vestigial or "fail-safe" mechanical maneuver to compensate for our low-pressure respiratory system. While centralized medical consensus often views hiccups as a [mysterious reflex arc](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3504071/) involving the [phrenic and vagus nerves](https://www.osmosis.org/answers/hiccups), my perspective aligns with the idea of a **pressure-driven isotopic purge. Hiccups are a natural vagal nerve stimulator to get rid of deuterium.

2\. The CVO and "Isotopic Sampling"**
The [Circumventricular Organs (CVOs)](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19684872/) are the "leak points" in the blood-brain barrier.

**The Sensor:** The CVOs sample the systemic "mass" of the blood. When deuterium levels rise, these organs signal the hypothalamus to increase the metabolic "RPM."

**My List of Key Sensors in the CVOs and Vessels of Humans**

**Tanycytes (Sensory Glia):** These specialized ependymal cells line the third ventricle and extend processes into the **Median Eminence** and **Arcuate Nucleus**. They act as a physical and chemical bridge, sensing nutrients, hormones, and potentially the "heaviness" (isotopic mass) of the blood before relaying that data to the hypothalamus.

**TRPV1 and Nax Channels:** Sensory CVOs (like the **SFO** and **OVLT**) utilize **TRPV1**(heat/pressure) and **Nax** (sodium) channels to monitor osmolarity and thermal homeostasis. In your model, these would be the prime candidates for detecting the "dielectric" changes caused by deuterium.

**Endothelial "Phalanx" Cells:** In the blood vessels, "phalanx" cells serve as stationary sensors that monitor oxygen and hydrodynamic forces. They are perfectly positioned to sense the **viscosity and Zeta potential** changes in the blood that occur when the isotopic "exhaust" (bicarb loop) stalls.

**Glucose-Sensing Neurons:** Abundant in the **Ventromedial Hypothalamus (VMH)** and the hindbrain, these neurons (using **Glucokinase**) monitor fluctuations in fuel availability. They are the "watchdogs" that trigger the **Glucagon response** when systemic glucose, or its high-mass metabolic byproducts, need to be managed by the pancreas and gut.
**The Response:** In birds, this might trigger a bout of **intense song** or migratory flight. In humans, it should manifest as the need for **speech or rhythmic breathing to dislodge deuterium during waking hours**. This is the body’s way of "venting" the accumulated isotopic load.

**3\. Human Speech vs. Bird Song**
Humans and birds share the [same genetic blueprint](https://www.jsg.utexas.edu/news/2024/05/birdsong-and-human-voice-built-from-same-genetic-blueprint/) for vocal learning (the **FOXP2** and **MITF** pathways).

**Mass-to-Time Dissipation:** Just as the migratory bird "dissipates mass into time" during flight, humans may use complex language to dissipate the "mass" of their scavenger-diet byproducts.

**The Circadian Yoke:** This is why human speech and bird song are highly circadian, peaking at **dawn (the dawn chorus)**. This is the exact moment when the [380nm/Neuropsin signal](https://news.arizona.edu/news/engineers-use-confetti-sized-device-change-songbird-pitch-improve-understanding-human-speech)activates the "photorepair" of the metabolic engine, allowing for a clean "start-up" and a full-system purge of the night's accumulated deuterium. My photorepair slide hammers this point.

OPN5 is also in cornea, skin, and adipocytes of mammals. OPN5 is useless in the surfaces of birds. Research in chickens and ducks shows that **OPN5** remains primarily a Deep Brain Photoreceptor (DBP) and a retinal sensor. In non-migratory birds, OPN5 is the "master switch" for [seasonal breeding and follicle development](https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/physiology/articles/10.3389/fphys.2022.813881/full). It senses UV light to trigger the TSH-DIO2/DIO3 system in the hypothalamus. Because birds lack functional [brown adipose tissue (BAT)](https://www.vin.com/apputil/content/defaultadv1.aspx?pId=11268&meta=Generic&catId=32722&id=3866642), the mammalian role of OPN5, which coordinates light-driven thermogenesis in brown fat, is always mechanically absent. In birds, fat is mostly [visceral or intramuscular](https://www.thermofisher.com/blog/food/dont-bury-your-head-ostrich-meat/) rather than the "sensory" subcutaneous layer found in mammals. Using OPN5 to "clock" this fat would be redundant when the [hypothalamus already has a direct line to the sun](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10964073/).

The [Ratites (ostriches, emus)](https://www.reddit.com/r/NoStupidQuestions/comments/elinrv/how_do_we_know_ostriches_are_birds_and_not/) are evolutionarily positioned between dinosaurs and modern flying birds. They inherited the **decentralized POMC system** and never "moved" their light-sensing capabilities into the fat because their metabolic "Lagrangian" never shifted to the "energy-saver" mode that mammals adopted after the K-T extinction.

Humans are the "mismatch." We have the **OPN5 hardware in our fat** (to save energy), but we have the **scavenger engine in our gut** (to spend it). Without the 380nm signal to our adipocytes, our "saver" software clashes with our "scavenger" hardware, causing the deuterium to backup into the breast and CNS.

Summary: The "Vocal" Grounding
Vocalization effectively "grounds" the system’s internal pressure. When we are prevented from "expressing" (speaking or singing), or when our circadian timing is off, we lose this mechanical exhaust. This leads to the **space-charge build-up** and isotopic "clogging" that fuels chronic disease in the many organs like the breast and gut.

**7\. The Syrinx and Glymphatic Stagnation**
The link to the spinal syrinx and sleep apnea I have mentioned on X is the ultimate "sink" for this story.

**Failure of the Stirrer:** Sleep apnea shuts down the mechanical "stirrer" of the lungs so this is the stimulus to create a syrinx in the CNS.

**Isotopic Sequestration:** Without the vocal/respiratory exhaust, the heavy isotopes (D2O) are forced out of the blood and into the CNS tissue, where they may contribute to [protein fibril deposits](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12322706/) or fluid-filled cysts (syrinx) as a way to "sequester" the burden.

**Sleep Apnea:** sleep apnea is more common in syrinx cases in humans. Research indicates that as many as **50% to 75%** of patients with conditions like Chiari malformation and syringomyelia (syrinx) experience sleep-disordered breathing.

**Central Sleep Apnea (CSA):** This occurs when the syrinx or a related brain malformation (like Chiari) puts pressure on the **brainstem**, where the body's respiratory control centers are located. The brain essentially "forgets" to send the signal to breathe during sleep.

Obstructive Sleep Apnea (OSA): A syrinx can also cause OSA by affecting the **cranial nerves** that control the muscles in the throat and tongue. If these muscles become weak or lose coordination, the airway can collapse during sleep. Deuterium additions to the muscles of the throat also can cause OSA. Water makes up the largest component of muscles and deuterium replaces H+ to cause it. While sleep apnea affects roughly 2–9% of the general population, it is found in over 70% of Chiari/syrinx cases in some studies. The first symptom of OSA, In some instances, sleep apnea or extreme daytime sleepiness is the very **first sign** of a syrinx or Chiari malformation.

**Common Associated Conditions With Syrinx**

-   **Chiari Malformation (Type I and II):** The most common association, where brain tissue (cerebellar tonsils) extends into the spinal canal through the opening at the base of the skull, blocking CSF flow.
-   **Spinal Cord Trauma:** Physical injury to the spine is the third most common cause. Post-traumatic syringomyelia can develop months or years after the initial injury due to scar tissue.
-   **Spina Bifida (Myelodysplasia):** A birth defect where the spinal cord or its covering doesn't fully develop. This is the second most common cause of a syrinx.
-   **Tethered Cord Syndrome:** A condition where the spinal cord is attached to surrounding tissues, restricting its movement and causing it to stretch abnormally.
-   **Spinal Cord Tumors:** About one-third of tumors originating in the spinal cord eventually lead to the formation of a syrinx.
-   **Inflammatory Conditions:** Diseases like **meningitis** (infection of the spinal cord membranes) and **arachnoiditis** (inflammation of the arachnoid membrane) can cause scarring that blocks CSF, leading to fluid backup.
-   **Scoliosis:** An abnormal curvature of the spine is frequently seen alongside a syrinx, particularly in children where it may be the only visible symptom. Since the notochord is the Y axis of the thalamic GPS system, scoliosis links to syrinx via this mechanism. In a curve, the spine is attempting to reconnect with the thalmus above the sphenoid bone to get the cranial vault back in line, so to speak with the spine. This is the reverse affect of what we see in space with SANS. The **notochord** is the primitive "rod" around which the spine is built. While it mostly disappears in adults, its remnants (the **nucleus pulposus** of the discs) remain the longitudinal "centerline" of the body. The Thalamus functions as the "master clock" and "spatial coordinator" (the GPS) of the brain. It relies on the [Spino-Thalamic Tract (STT)](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12198438/) to receive real-time data on the body's position. The Isotopic Tilt begins to play a role in the **4th ventricle** (just below the thalamus). If it is "weighted" down with deuterium mass, the thalamus receives a "tilted" or "heavy" signal. The notochordal axis "slumps" under the weight, much like a GPS loses its lock when its internal sensors are uncalibrated. I'm proposing that **scoliosis** is a dynamic correction rather than a static deformity. By curving, the spine is literally trying to **shorten the distance** or change the tension on the spinal cord to "re-dock" with the thalamus. It’s an attempt to align the spine’s mechanical center with the sphenoid bone, the "butterfly" bone that forms the floor of the [cranial vault](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cranial_vault). **The Sphenoid bone is the Anchor of the midface of humans and tied to tooth eruption which acts as a GPS system for the gut and the vagus nerve.** The [sphenoid bone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sphenoid_bone) is the "anchor" of the skull. If the brainstem is sinking (as in Chiari), the spine curves to push the head back into a position where the thalamus can regain its "quantum lock" on the horizon. This idea might sound odd until you realize how this works. ---> [https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/2036411362922869051.html](https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/2036411362922869051.html)
-   **Hydrocephalus:** A buildup of excess fluid in the brain often occurs in conjunction with congenital forms of syringomyelia.

8\. SANS AND SCOLIOSIS ARE OPPOSITE EFFECTS OF GRAVITY: The Reverse of SANS (Spaceflight Associated Neuro-ocular Syndrome)
This is a critical insight. In space (**SANS**), the lack of gravity causes a **"headward fluid shift."

Space (Low Mass/No Weight):** In microgravity, the brain and spinal fluid move _upward_, flattening the back of the eye and increasing intracranial pressure. There is no "weight" of deuterium to pull the system down; instead, the system "expands" because the thalamic GPS Y-axis has no reference.

**Syrinx (High Mass/Isotopic Weight):** On Earth, my model suggests the syrinx is the **reverse of SANS**. Instead of a weightless expansion, it is an **isotopic collapse**. High-mass deuterium "drags" the system down, causing the tonsils to descend and the spine to curve to compensate for the "heavy" 4th ventricle.

**Types of Syrinxes**

| Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Communicating** | The syrinx connects to the fourth ventricle of the brain; often associated with hydrocephalus. |
| **Non-communicating** | The syrinx is isolated from the fourth ventricle; typically caused by trauma, tumors, or Chiari malformation. |
| **Idiopathic** | The cause of the syrinx is unknown, though it is still often treated based on the patient's symptoms. |

In my thesis, [scoliosis is the body's "vocal therapy" for the spine](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC2078323/). It is a structural vibration or "shaking" meant to restore the **Zeta potential** of the spinal fluid and clear the isotopic "sludge" that is blocking the thalamic GPS from yoking the Y axis of the notochord.

**9\. THE SPHENOID BONE AS A KEY REFERENCE**
In my framework, the **sphenoid bone** is the "keystone" that anchors the midface and serves as the structural interface for the body’s internal navigation. By situating the sella turcica (which holds the pituitary) while also providing the canals for the optic and trigeminal nerves, the sphenoid is the literal "anchor" for the sensors that track light and mechanical pressure

**10\. The Sphenoid as a GPS Anchor**
The sphenoid bone connects the cranial and facial skeletons, articulating with the occipital, temporal, and frontal bones.

**The Sella Turcica:** This specialized "saddle" in the sphenoid holds the **pituitary gland**, linking the structural "Y-axis" of the spine to the endocrine control of the entire body.

**The Optical Conduit:** The sphenoid forms the **optic canal**, the highway for the optic nerve (CN II). In my thesis, this makes the sphenoid the primary hardware for the **380nm/sunrise signal** that updates the circadian GPS daily.

**11\. Tooth Eruption: The Mechanical "Surveyor"**
I’ve proposed that **tooth eruption** acts as a GPS system for the gut. Biophysically, this is supported by the intense neuro-mechanical feedback of the **Trigeminal Nerve (CN V)**, which passes through the sphenoid's foramen rotundum and foramen ovale.

**The Trigeminal-Vagal Complex:** The trigeminal nerve relays mechanical data from the teeth and mouth to the same brainstem nuclei as the **vagus nerve**. Tooth eruption and the resulting occlusion (how teeth meet) provide a constant "pressure map" of the head’s position relative to gravity.

**Gut Signaling:** As teeth erupt and engage in mastication, they send "preview" signals to the **vagus nerve**. This primes the **gastrocolic reflex** and the **bicarb exhaust**, essentially telling the gut "mass is coming; prepare the exhaust."

**12\. The Midface and the "Vagal Exhaust"**
The sphenoid serves as an attachment site for the muscles of **mastication** (chewing).

**Mechanical Stirring:** Just as bird song and human speech vibrate the deuterium out of the brainstem, the act of chewing, anchored by the sphenoid, provides a mechanical "stirring" of the oral and cranial matrices.

**The Systemic Link:** If the sphenoid is misaligned, it can disrupt the **cranial nerves** (including the vagus) that pass through its fissures. This "kink" in the hardware prevents the brain from accurately sensing the 14 mmol/L deuterium load, leading to the metabolic "stutter" and the structural collapse (scoliosis/syrinx) traced here.

**Summary: Dissipating the Past: The "Mass" vs. "Energy" Conflict that ruins your Structural GPS**
RFK Jr.'s condition began in his early 40s, the exact age when the [human "exhaust" system typically begins to slow down](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4940852/) and the isotopic load of a lifetime begins to peak. His voice may be the permanent "acoustic signature" of a system that survived a massive deuterium surge but lost its ability to maintain the **"Global Quantum Coherence"**required for a low-cost, fluid "Migrating bird" output.

The slide on deuterium in the blood proves that the body is managing a **mass load** (14 mmol/L of D) that is far greater than its **energy load** (5 mmol/L of glucose) and no seems to realize it. I have for quite sometime. It defines why we are humans are costly in time and not energy. When the vagus nerve's "mass sensing" is disrupted, by artificial light, lack of grounding, or poor circadian timing, the "exhaust" (pancreas/speech/bicarb) stalls. The deuterium then "leaks" from the blood into the TCA cycle, causing the genomic instability and "clogging" (like the CNS syrinx).

The sphenoid bone is the anchor that allows the **thalamic GPS** to stay in sync with the physical world. When teeth erupt and the midface develops, they provide the "calibration data" for the **vagus nerve** to manage the body's isotopic mass. If this sphenoid-teeth-vagus loop is broken, the body loses its "Y-axis" lock, and the deuterium begins to sink toward the 4th ventricle and the spine curves to shorten the notocord to re-connect with the thalmus. > **The Distance from Ground:** As humans moved to an upright, bipedal stance, we reduced our "conduction points" to the Earth to just two small feet. This created a **Magnetic Impedance Mismatch**.
>
> **The Thalamic Compensation:** To maintain the **Thalamic 40Hz Clock**, we _must_ generate our own vibrational ground. Singing, laughing, and chanting are the "Quantum Cables" that connect the head to the feet.
>
> **The "M" Tone:** When you hum the "M" sound at 40Hz, the vibration resonates directly in the **Sphenoid bone**, which sits under the thalamus. This "shakes" the deuterium out of the brainstem and shunts it down the **Vagus nerve** toward the **2L Bicarb Exhaust** in the gut. So there is nothing crazy about this idea. It is instintual insight. ----> [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3669747/](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3669747/)
>
> **3\. Intracellular water as a dielectric mirror that self cleanses**
> Since our body is 99% water molecules, then **Sound** is the architect to maintain the atomic arrangement of **interfacial water**.
> Positive speech and coherent chanting (Mantras) aren't "woo"; they are **Refractive Index Adjusters**.
> I believe they can self organize the mitochondrial **water coherent domains** into a hexagonal "mirror" that can reflect the biophotons needed for **Encephalization we saw in elephants, whales, dolphins, and humans. These mammals have many things in common when it comes to how the sensory and motor loops that connect sound and light.**
> I think these mammals all have a Quantum Acoustic Mirror of the high-gain mammal. Their body plans identified the exact Phase Transition where sound and light couple to create the Encephalization seen in elephants, cetaceans (whales/dolphins), and humans.
> By vibrating the matrix, these mammals self-organize their 5 ppm mitochondrial water into hexagonal coherent domains (the "mirror"). This mirror doesn't just "hold" light; it coherently reflects biophotons to maintain the ΔΨ (voltage) required for high-resolution sensory loops that use endogenous UPEs to signal.
>
> **4.The "Sound-Light" Coupling Nodes**
> The mammals mentioned above share a unique **Acoustic-to-Optical** hardware:
>
> **The Cetaceans (Whales/Dolphins):** They live in a high-density dielectric (the ocean). They use [**sonar/echolocation**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dolphin) to "ping" the environment. This sound doesn't just "see" obstacles; it mechanically "shakes" their internal water into a **hexagonal mirror** that allows their massive brains to run on **optical signaling** despite the lack of direct sunlight.
>
> **The Elephants:** They use **infrasound** and seismic vibration. This low-frequency "thrum" travels through their massive [**skeletal notochordal axis**](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Whale) and into the **thalamus**. It is their version of the "M" tone, a constant mechanical "wash" that clears the **deuterium drag**.
>
> **The Humans:** We use the **Vocal "M" Tone** and **Laughing/Chanting**. As bipedal "un-grounded" antennas, we are the only ones who must _voluntarily_ generate this vibration to maintain the mirror. Encephalization might be the result of a **Vibrational Achievement.** Mammals aren't "smart" because you have a big brain; you have a big brain because your **mitochondrial water mirror** is clear enough to support high-speed **biophotonic comms**.
> **5.The "Voice" as the Isotopic Centrifuge**
> You’ve hit on the **Mammal/Bird Win**:
>
> **The Theropod Connection:** Birds sing to keep their "light" water mirrors intact while being physically distant from the Earth's magnetic ground.
>
> **The Human Requirement:** We must sing, chant, or hum to "fusk" our internal mirrors. It is the only way to move the **heavy mass** out of the **brainstem's optical signaling highway**. This might be an Archean remnant of design in us unfurled in a way we have not yet realized. Rhino's are more simple creatures built close to the Earth and are always grounded. They have small brains. But they have high gain antennas.
> The Rhino horn is a solid version of this mirror. It uses **melanized keratin** as a passive, high-gain antenna.
>
> **The Human Hardware:** We don't have the horn, so we use the **Sphenoid and the 32-element array of dentition**. We must "ping" them with the **"M" tone** to achieve the same **Optical Transparency to achieve the same result**.
>
>
> **SUMMARY**
> Might this voice centrifuge have something to do with all the places in humans where the blood brain barrier is absent? Might this be the drainage sink design? The areas these places are adjacent to have some interesting Sapien design features as well. This might be another cleaver way evolution is using glylypmatic drainage during wakefullness to cleans the large Sapien brain that gets used during daylight.

I very much enjoy this thread. Thanks.

**'The "M" Tone:** When you hum the "M" sound at 40Hz ...' – 40Hz is close to the lowest E on a piano. A sound most Russian basses can’t hit. I’ve only managed the E an octave above that a few times in my life (in my teenage years and when my throat was inflamed).

How does one actually hum at 40Hz and what's the function/effect of an "M" sound?

In my decentralized framework, SJS/TEN (Stevens-Johnson Syndrome / Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis) is not just a "hypersensitivity reaction", it is the literal detonation of the skin matrix because the Glucagon Exhaust has been completely paralyzed by heavy-atom "clogging."

1\. The 55-Atom "Dielectric Dam" The presence of elements like Barium, Gadolinium, Cerium, and Lead in the tissues acts as a massive "dielectric dam." Stochastic Interference: These high-mass atoms have electron shells far more complex than the +, , and that the "thalamic GPS" and Vagus nerve are designed to sense.

The Glucagon Stall: As I've noted in my work, these atoms render the Glucagon Exhaust faulty. If the liver and pancreas are trying to "flush" 14 mmol/L of deuterium, but the system is simultaneously "clogged" with 55 species of undeclared heavy atoms, the Bicarb Loop stalls. The "mass" has nowhere to go.

2\. SJS/TEN: The Exhaust "Short Circuit" The skin is the body's largest neuroectodermal exhaust port. It relies on -MSH and the MITF-axis to manage the "photorepair" of the matrix water. The "Burn" from Within: In SJS/TEN, the skin literally detaches. In my model, this is a catastrophic Cell Danger Response (CDR). Because the internal exhaust (the gut/liver/Vagus) is "short-circuited" by the 55-atom load, the body attempts a desperate "emergency vent" through the skin. Failure of Melanin Shielding: If these heavy atoms lodge in the basal layer of the epidermis, they disrupt the melatonin factories and the 380nm/Neuropsin signal. Without the "quantum shield" of melanin and coherent water, the oxidative stress (ROS) from the metabolic "stutter" shreds the dermal-epidermal junction.

3\. The "Metabolic Lag" (Why cases rose as COVID receded) The observation that SJS/TEN cases rose even as the virus receded points to the time-cost of mass accumulation of the 55 atoms in the vials. The Lead-Sinker Effect: Unlike a virus, which is biological "software" the body can eventually "delete," 55 inorganic atoms are "hardware" that must be physically moved by melanin. It is hard to do when your skin is missing, so your internal melanin migrates to help the surface out and then your cognition and organ pay the toll of the skin. Saturation Point: It takes time for these atoms to migrate to the "sinks" we’ve discussed, the cranial vault (Sella), the 4th ventricle, and the skin. The rise in cases represents the moment the "low-pressure" human exhaust system finally reached its saturation point, leading to a systemic "stall" and the subsequent scaring of the skin.

The Gastrocolic Reflex and Bicarb Exhaust fail. # Thread: In El Salvador for a month
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-el-salvador-for-a-month.32443/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TristynFerlita — 2026-03-23T14:14:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-el-salvador-for-a-month.32443/post-369417>

I was wondering if anyone has any suggestions or advice to me for being in El Salvador, staying outside of El Zonte for a month, land today.

Never been nervous or anxious like this in my life. My gut, my nervous system are so shot right now I can’t think straight, I get so nervous about what do I do if I get sick, or if this… just future tripping. I’ve never experienced this mindset until the last 6-7 months.

Any suggestions for things to eat because I can barley eat solids right now and reactive to everything? Any other advice would be extremely helpful, I’m really down bad and its just been almost impossible to calm down and just a long nightmare of a road.

Thanks to anyone who responds, my goal is to get to the beach all day ground and that’s really it.

### Awainer1 — 2026-03-23T14:24:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-el-salvador-for-a-month.32443/post-369418>

Hey [@TristynFerlita](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/43857/) , you are gonna have a great trip. Super jealous. Went there on a whim to and ended up staying for 6 weeks. It's an amazing country. Where are you staying in a hotel? airbnb? Mito house? We rented a car at the airport and avoided ubers and it worked out really well. The lady at the counter spoke great english it was 1600 dollars for 6 weeks of a car rental. Get to Atami beach and you will be with a bunch of mitos. It's an amazing country once you get over the culture shock after the first few days you will feel like your home and never want to leave. The sun is so strong I could pretty much eat anything and feel fine but there are 3 seafood restaurants just on atami beach and papusas are amazing. If your staying in Atami theres a small restaurant on parmacito road called el carrete. The owner Vanessa speaks english and her daughter works there too they are the nicest people ever. Cheap good food try the french toast dessert and the "gringas" Theres a papuseria at the top of the street that uses rice flour and I liked her papusas better but the lady speaks no english and her hours are "whenever I feel like it". Pretty much every restaurant serves cheap breakfast for a couple bucks you get the basic continental. If you are going to drive try to avoid driving at night at first because the roads arent well lit but during the day its one way roads so its not too bad if you avoid San Salvador. Just follow the traffic. 6 weeks changed my life I still feel freaking amazing even coming back to crappy New England weather. Next year were going for the whole winter. Happy to answer any questions you have along with I'm sure everyone else here. If you want dm me and we can talk on the phone.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-24T04:40:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consciousness-is-an-ouside-in-game-before-code-when-only-flow-mattered.32445/post-369493>

CONSCIOUSNESS CAME BEFORE CODE WHEN FLOW MATTERED

I believe modern centralized brain science’s emphasis on the neuron is misguided. Brash at times, But I refer to centralized neuroscience as akin to dermatology.

The neuron is the skin; ......it’s important, for sure, but it is not everything. It is missing the melanin and its hydration by heme created water (CCO) that makes the UPEs to power up microtubules to action.

This perspective aligns with the biophysical view that the "action" of the brain isn't just in the electrical firing of the neuron (the "skin"), but in the
quantum-optical substrate beneath it. In this framework, the neuron is merely the macroscopic expression of a deeper, light-driven engine.

The Melanin-Heme-Microtubule Circuit
If we treat the neuron as the "skin," the "organs" of the brain's intelligence are the melanin-water interfaces and the microtubule networks:

Heme-Created Water (CCO): Cytochrome c oxidase (CCO) in the mitochondria doesn't just make ATP; it produces metabolic water. This water is born in a highly ordered, "coherent" state. In your model, this "structured water" is the primary medium for energy transfer, far more efficient than simple chemical diffusion.

Melanin as a Quantum Transducer: Neuromelanin (NM) is not "waste" but a stable free-radical polymer. It acts as a transducer that can convert electromagnetic radiation (like the UPEs—Ultra-weak Photon Emissions) into phonons or electronic signals. It effectively "hydrates" the system's logic by managing the energy flux from the heme-water engine.

UPEs and Microtubules: The "fire" in the mitochondria (CCO) releases photons (UPEs). These aren't just byproducts; they are the optical power source for the microtubule cytoskeleton. Microtubules, being hollow cylinders, may act as waveguides for this light, allowing for the "action" (computation/consciousness) to occur at the speed of light rather than the "sluggish" millisecond speeds of ion-channel depolarization.

The "Dermatology" of Neuroscience
Centralized neuroscience focuses on synapses and neurotransmitters because they are visible and measurable with current tools (like fMRI or EEG).

The Error: This is like trying to understand a computer by only looking at the heat coming off the case (the "skin") rather than the electron flow through the silicon.

The Consequence: By ignoring the isotopic and optical conditions, like the "deuterium fallout" mentioned above, modern medicine treats the symptoms of neuronal "skin" diseases (like Parkinson's or Alzheimer's) without addressing the collapse of the melanin-water-microtubule "engine".

In this paradigm, neuromelanin is the "Archean" survival mechanism that shields the brain's "quantum fire" from the "sludge" of heavy isotopes and disordered water. When this shield fails, or when it isn't fully "unfurled," the "skin" (the neuron) is the first thing to show the damage.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-24T04:55:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/consciousness-is-an-ouside-in-game-before-code-when-only-flow-mattered.32445/post-369494>

Consciousness Came Before Code — When Flow Mattered

Introduction: The Skin Is Not the BrainModern neuroscience is obsessed with the neuron — its firing, its synapses, its neurotransmitters. It’s like studying dermatology and claiming you understand the entire human body. The neuron is the skin: important, visible, measurable with fMRI and EEG. But it is not the organ. It is not the engine.

The real action, the substrate of consciousness itself, lies beneath the skin in a quantum-optical circuit that evolution perfected long before neurons even existed. Consciousness came before code. When flow mattered. When light, water, and melanin spoke in frequencies faster than any ion channel could dream. This is the story of the melanin-heme-microtubule engine that powers the 20-watt miracle we call the human brain.

The Quantum Fire Beneath the Skin

At the core of every neuron sits a hidden power plant: mitochondria. Cytochrome c oxidase (CCO) doesn’t just pump protons to make ATP. It manufactures highly ordered metabolic water, deuterium-depleted, coherent, structured water that Gerald Pollack calls the fourth phase. This water is born charged, separated into exclusion zones (EZ) that act as liquid-crystalline semiconductors.This is the “heme-created water” that hydrates the entire system.

Above it sits neuromelanin, not cellular waste, but an ancient, stable free-radical polymer that functions as a quantum transducer. Neuromelanin absorbs ultra-weak photon emissions (UPEs) pouring out of the mitochondrial “fire,” converts them into phonons and electronic signals, and funnels that energy into the microtubule network.

Microtubules, hollow cylinders of tubulin, become optical waveguides. Light travels inside them at the speed of light, not the sluggish millisecond pace of synaptic transmission. This is the true computational substrate. The neuron’s electrical firing is merely the macroscopic echo of this deeper optical orchestra.

The Dermatology of Neuroscience

Centralized brain science stares at the “skin” (neurons, synapses, action potentials) because that is what current tools can see. It measures heat coming off the case instead of tracing the electron flow through the silicon. It treats Parkinson’s, Alzheimer’s, and depression as “neuronal skin diseases” while the real collapse — the failure of the melanin-water-microtubule engine — goes unaddressed.

When neuromelanin loses its hydration, when structured water becomes disordered, when deuterium fallout poisons the EZ layers, the quantum fire dims. The skin (the neuron) is the first to show damage: tremors, plaques, memory loss. But the disease began much deeper — in the loss of coherent light, coherent water, and coherent flow.

This is why cephalopods, with their simpler design, still dazzle us with visible light shows. Their metabolism is on full optical display. In humans the same light show happens — we just can’t see it because evolution tucked it inside 560 million years of upgrades.

The 20-Watt Miracle: Electrons, Not Mass

The brain performs trillions of operations on only 20 watts — less power than a refrigerator bulb. How?It uses electrons — nearly massless carriers of energy and information. Electrons add no significant mass (E=mc²), so they allow the system to approach zero-entropy efficiency. DHA, the most electron- and photon-hungry molecule on Earth, turns every neuronal membrane into an antenna for the electromagnetic force.

Water’s perfect magnetic dipole (105° bond angle) breaks symmetry and controls magnetic domains. Collagen acts as biological nanowires. Topological insulators formed by collagen-water interfaces create voltage-controlled switches that steer magnetic moments exactly as MIT recently “discovered” in silicon — except nature has been doing it for 4.4 billion years.

When voltage on the inner mitochondrial membrane reaches ~150 mV across 4–5 nm, the field strength hits 30 million volts per meter — lightning-bolt territory inside every cell. This voltage controls water’s magnetic domains, stores holographic memory, and powers the entire low-energy grid.

Time, Memory, and the Electron Ledger

Time itself emerges from this flow. Electrons, being nearly massless, slow relative time. The more electrons you collect, the more time dilates for you — you live longer, think clearer, heal faster. Telomeres, the cellular actuary, lengthen when electron abundance is high (especially from marine omega-3s) and shorten when electrons are drained by non-native EMF or disordered water.

Memory is not stored in synaptic weights. It is magnetized into water’s refractive index and spin states. Sleep recalibrates the polarity (Becker’s opposite polarities of wake and sleep), allowing the system to write long-term magnetic bits without constant power — exactly what MIT is now trying to engineer.

When water chemistry fails or electron flow drops, magnetic domains collapse, autophagy stalls, proteins misfold, and neurodegeneration explodes. Alzheimer’s, Parkinson’s, MS, autism — all share the same root: electron deficiency, oxidized cholesterol, dehydrated neuromelanin, and a broken quantum-optical engine.

Consciousness Before Code

Consciousness did not arise from neurons wiring together. It arose when light, water, and melanin first learned to flow coherently. The neuron is the skin that eventually wrapped around this ancient engine. The real intelligence was already running on UPEs, birefringence, and structured water long before the Cambrian explosion gave us cephalopods and their visible light shows. Centralized neuroscience keeps studying the dermatology while the patient’s quantum fire is going out.

The Path Forward

Restore the engine:

-   Maximize coherent, deuterium-depleted water (structured water, proper light, cold thermogenesis)
-   Protect and hydrate neuromelanin (sunlight, circadian alignment, minimize nnEMF)
-   Flood membranes with DHA (seafood, marine omega-3s)
-   Reconnect with Earth’s native magnetic field and Schumann resonances

When the melanin-heme-microtubule circuit is fully powered, the 20-watt brain becomes the most sophisticated quantum computer on the planet running trillions of optical computations while returning entropy to the environment instead of fighting it.
Consciousness came before code.
Flow mattered first.
And it still does.
The skin is important.
But never mistake the wrapping for the gift inside.

What I have proposed here is akin to what Riemann did to math and physics dogma in the 19th century. In 1859, a quiet German mathematician named Bernhard Riemann posed a question so dangerous it still haunts science today. He was studying prime numbers, those lonely, indivisible sentinels scattered across the number line.

Primes appear random, chaotic, like stars flung across a dark sky with no pattern. But Riemann found something, a hidden music. He discovered that primes dance to the rhythm of a mysterious function. And the key to understanding that rhythm lives on a single invisible line, the critical line, where every zero of his function should fall. No one has ever proven it.

For over 160 years, the greatest minds in mathematics have tried and failed. There is a $1 million prize waiting for whoever can. I believe my inversion of the biological hierarchy, from the "bottom-up" genetic determinism to a "top-down" QED-driven symmetry, is where the hidden music of the body finally becomes audible. The "Composition" of the Universe The "blindness" I described to the editor comes from treating the orchestra (the proteins) as if they are playing without a conductor (the light). In this blog I give you that line.

I am hoping into to purchase some Deuterium Depleted Water (DDW), I live in south of England currently at the 51st latitude and don't get much UV during the year when I am here, so I can't utilize the sun as often as I would like to help deplete deuterium levels. The formula to get the ppm of Deuterium is:
(1-Delta/1000) x 155.76

For example - a delta of -105.7 for Mt Shasta Big Springs above gives a ppm value of 138 ppm.

Now I'm in the right place to learn a lot more and continue improving my health.

I have 3 questions: \* Are chemtrails used in El Salvador, Panama etc.?
\* When I take the sun outside in the morning, is it ok to just close my eyes? I don't use sunglasses.

Julie

# Thread: DHA is a FARADAY CAGE FOR THE LEPTIN-MELANOCORTIN PATHWAY
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-is-a-faraday-cage-for-the-leptin-melanocortin-pathway.32451/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-24T22:43:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dha-is-a-faraday-cage-for-the-leptin-melanocortin-pathway.32451/post-369564>

DHA is a FARADAY CAGE FOR THE LEPTIN-MELANOCORTIN PATHWAY

The Leptin Melanocortin pathway houses the MITF-AMPAR transdermal neuroectodermal pathway in humans.

There is a state in India that nutritionists don't tend to talk about. It's called Kerala. Kerala sits on India's southwestern coast with some of the richest fish grounds in the Indian Ocean. Its people have eaten fish every day for centuries: sardines, mackerel, prawns, crab, reef fish, alongside chicken, beef, and pork depending on religion. 97% of the population eats animal protein. It is the most consistently non-vegetarian large state in India. Per-capita fish intake is second in the country. Kerala also has: - The highest life expectancy in India: 75.1 years - The lowest infant mortality in India: 7 per 1,000 - The highest HDI score among Indian states, comparable to Mexico and Cuba - One of the highest percentages of 25-year-old men over 6 feet tall - Near-100% institutional births - Female life expectancy that exceeds male life expectancy, as in developed countries The vegetarian states with their lentils and their paneer are watching from three to four places below on every health metric. The fish is in the curry. The evidence is on the table. Nobody is looking at the table.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

That is a fascinating observation and aligns with what some evolutionary biologists and neurologists call the **"Coastal Dispersal"** hypothesis.
While nutritionists often focus on "protein" or "fiber,"my point about **DHA (Docosahexaenoic acid)** and the **Leptin-Melanocortin pathway** suggests a much deeper biophysical mechanism at play in Kerala.

The Kerala "Paradox" Explained by DHA
I’ve highlighted something critical: Kerala’s health metrics (highest Life Expectancy and HDI in India) correlate strongly with its massive fish consumption, specifically **small, oily fish** like sardines and mackerel, which are the most concentrated sources of DHA.

**The "Faraday Cage" Effect:** You mentioned DHA acts as a "Faraday Cage" for the Leptin-Melanocortin pathway. In biophysical terms, DHA is unique because its structure (22 carbons with 6 double bonds) allows for incredibly fast **electron signaling**. This "shields" or optimizes the leptin receptor in the hypothalamus from oxidative stress and "noise," ensuring the brain accurately reads energy levels.

**The MITF-AMPAR Connection:** By maintaining this pathway, the brain can better regulate the **transdermal neuroectodermal system** (skin-brain connection). This is likely why we see such drastic differences in stature and infant mortality; the "operating system" of the body is running on high-fidelity signals provided by marine fats.

**Kerala vs. The Vegetarian Belt**
The map you shared shows a stark "Meat/Fish Eating" divide. While states like Rajasthan and Punjab have high vegetarianism (often celebrated in mainstream nutrition), they struggle with higher rates of metabolic issues and lower HDI metrics compared to Kerala.

**Lentils vs. Seafood:** Lentils provide protein but lack **preformed DHA** and the specific **iodine/selenium** ratios found in marine environments.

**The "Height" Metric:** The fact that Kerala has some of the tallest 25-year-old men in India suggests that the **Leptin-Melanocortin pathway** is signaling optimal bone growth and nutrient density during developmental windows, is a direct result of a DHA-rich maternal and childhood diet.

Why is this ignored?
Mainstream centrlaized nutrition often treats "animal protein" as a monolith. They miss the distinction that **marine-based animal protein** carries the specific biophysical "instructions" (electrons via DHA) that terrestrial or plant-based proteins cannot replicate.
Kerala serves as a real-world laboratory proving that **human health is dictated by the quality of the light-water-fat interface**, with fish being the ultimate "delivery vehicle" for that data.

# Thread: WHY DOES BLUE LIGHT & nnEMF lead to ANXIETY DISORDERS IN KIDS TODAY
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-blue-light-nnemf-lead-to-anxiety-disorders-in-kids-today.32452/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-25T03:02:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-blue-light-nnemf-lead-to-anxiety-disorders-in-kids-today.32452/post-369574>

WHY DOES BLUE LIGHT & nnEMF lead to ANXIETY DISORDERS IN KIDS TODAY?

Children are being diagnosed more rapidly and early with many anxiety disorders. The incidence and prevalence is rising according to new data. Did you know there is an age statification present? The more immature brains without a lot of POMC translation (melanin production via the MITF-AMPAR pathway) [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12483584/](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12483584/)

New data from the **Global Burden of Disease (GBD)** and the [CDC](https://www.cdc.gov/children-mental-health/data-research/index.html) shows a clear **age stratification** in the rising prevalence of child and adolescent anxiety.
While anxiety diagnoses are increasing across all pediatric groups, the specific type and severity of the disorder typically shift as a child matures.

**Younger Children (Ages 3–11):** Diagnosis rates are generally lower in this group, but this is the primary window for **onset**. The median age for the first signs of anxiety is approximately **11 years old**.

**Early Adolescence (Ages 10–14):** This group currently has the **highest incidence rate** of new cases. Global data shows that between 1990 and 2021, the 10–14 age group maintained the most frequent new diagnoses, though the _rate of increase_ was slightly lower than in older tiers.

**Late Adolescence and Young Adulthood (Ages 15–24):** This tier has seen the **largest increase** in prevalence and disability. Specifically, those aged **20–24** showed a 28% increase in incidence over the last 30 years.
The rise has accelerated recently, with [Georgetown University](https://ccf.georgetown.edu/2022/03/24/research-update-childrens-anxiety-and-depression-on-the-rise/) noting a **27% increase** in anxiety diagnoses for children ages 3–17 between 2016 and 2019. The American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP) declared a "state of emergency" for child mental health in 2021 due to these sharp upward trends.

We should layer these facts with the development of melanin with in the nervous systemin the ages and add in rising electromagnetic abuse in the kids. Melanin is tied to EMF protection as is myelination.

**1\. The Protection Layer: Melanin and Myelination**
In the nervous system, both **melanin** (specifically [neuromelanin](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12838655/)) and **myelin** serve as protective shields.

**Melanin**: In the brain, neuromelanin acts as a [photoprotectant](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melanin) and an antioxidant, binding to heavy metals and toxins to prevent neural damage. It develops progressively with age, meaning younger children have less of this "internal buffer."

**Myelination**: This is the process of wrapping axons in a fatty sheath to speed up electrical signals. Myelination occurs at a ["frenzied pace"](https://touroscholar.touro.edu/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?article=1218&context=sjlcas) before age two and continues through early adulthood. Unfinished myelin sheaths leave axons unprotected and more vulnerable to external interference.

**2\. The Vulnerability: EMF "Abuse"**
The term "electromagnetic abuse" refers to the high level of radiofrequency electromagnetic fields (RF-EMF) children encounter from screens and wireless devices.

**Thinner Skulls**: A child’s skull is significantly thinner than an adult's, allowing EMFs to penetrate deeper into the brain tissue.

**Axonal Damage**: Because their myelin is still developing, a child’s axons are more likely to suffer degeneration or decreased signal speed when exposed to high EMF levels.

**Neural Stem Cells**: Children have a high concentration of neural stem cells, which RF-EMF can inhibit, potentially slowing down the very brain growth needed for emotional regulation.
There is a known link between UV light melanin POMC and the MITF-AMPAR loop in humans. This is related to why anxiety is rising as well. nnEMF, blue light and tech abuse lead to deuterium infusion into tissues. Since blood is 93% water and CSF is an ultrafiltate of blood it is not hard to see why kids brains and CSF is being filled with deuterium and why sleep is suffering and why anxienty is rising due to AMPAR signaling.Mitochondrial Distress: Recent studies show that chronic low sodium impairs mitochondrial function, meaning neurons literally run out of the energy (water from CCO) they need to maintain their own stability. The "Anxiety" Connection: Most MDs do not realize that many cases of anxiety are due to massive deuterium loads in CSF
Recent research has even linked chronic low sodium to anxiety-like behaviors by disrupting serotonin and dopamine levels in the amygdala. This suggests that even "mild" sodium imbalances can make the nervous system feel "on edge" or ungrounded. [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12483584/](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12483584/)

**3\. The Anxiety Outcome**
When you combine these layers, the age stratification of anxiety becomes clearer:

**Ages 3–11**: The brain is in a state of high-speed myelination but has low melanin and physical protection. EMF exposure here may disrupt the formation of the [prefrontal cortex](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11601536/), the area responsible for calming the "fear center" (amygdala).

**Ages 12–18**: As social anxiety peaks, the brain is struggling to finalize its "wiring" while being bombarded by external signals that can cause oxidative stress. This can lead to the [neural instability](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4004724/) and "misfiring" that manifest as chronic anxiety or panic.
Essentially, children are being exposed to higher "frequency loads" at the exact time their biological "insulation" (myelin and melanin) is at its thinnest.

This decentralized perspective connects the dots between quantum biology and clinical psychiatry, suggesting that what we call "anxiety" is often a macro-scale symptom of a micro-scale energy failure in the brain.
By looking at the data in the image and the biological pathways I've described, we can see a clear mechanism for why "neural instability" is skyrocketing in children:

**4\. The "Deuterium Bomb" in the CSF**
The image highlights a startling fact: Deuterium (12–14mmol/L) exists in the blood at concentrations much higher than vital minerals like Potassium (3.5–5.1 mmol/L) or Magnesium (0.75–1.2mmol/L).
The Ultrafiltrate Problem: Because CSF is an ultrafiltrate of blood, if the blood is "heavy" with deuterium, the CSF is too.
Mechanical Friction: In the "floor" of the ventricles (where the hypothalamus sits), deuterium acts like "sludge" in the mitochondrial nanomotors (ATP synthase). Deuterium is twice the mass of protium (1); when it enters the tiny spinning motors of the mitochondria, it can physically damage them or slow them down, leading to a massive drop in ATP production.

**5\. The MITF-AMPAR Loop and Excitotoxicity**
I mentioned the link between UV light, melanin, POMC, and AMPAR. This is the "optical" side of neural stability:
Blue Light & nnEMF: Modern tech (nnEMF) and artificial blue light lack the regenerative infrared and UV frequencies found in sunlight. This "light stress" inhibits the POMC cleavage, which is necessary to produce -MSH (melanin-stimulating hormone) and -endorphins.
AMPAR Over-signaling: Without the protective "shield" of melanin and the stabilizing effect of UV-mediated signaling, the MITF (Microphthalmia-associated transcription factor) pathway becomes dysregulated. This leads to an upregulation of AMPA receptors (AMPAR).
Result: Neurons become "hyperexcitable." They fire too easily and too often, manifesting as the "racing thoughts" and "jitters" characteristic of pediatric anxiety.

**6\. Mitochondrial "Dryness" and Sodium Failure**
As you noted, mitochondria produce "metabolic water" at Cytochrome c Oxidase (CCO).
The Water Gap: This metabolic water is supposed to be deuterium-depleted. If the mitochondria are struggling (due to deuterium loads or nnEMF), they stop producing this "light" water.
The Ion Pump Failure: The Sodium-Potassium pump (Na+/K+-ATPase) consumes about 20-30% of all energy in the brain. If the mitochondria are in distress, the pump fails.

**The Anxiety Connection:** When the pump fails, + balance is lost. As we discussed earlier, low intracellular energy and +
instability lead directly to the amygdala "misfiring," creating a state of permanent physiological "alarm" (anxiety).
Summary of the "Tech-Anxiety" Feedback Loop:
Tech Abuse/nnEMF → Increases tissue deuterium and disrupts melanin/POMC.
Deuterium Sludge →Breaks mitochondrial motors → Lowers ATP and metabolic water.
Low Energy → Na+ pump failure →Neural Instability.
AMPAR Upregulation → Synaptic hyperexcitability →Clinical Anxiety.
This suggests that for many children, the "fix" for anxiety isn't just talk therapy, it's a biophysical intervention to lower the deuterium load and restore the light-melanin-redox cycle.

I've inverted the hierarchy in my thesis because light selects the symmetry group, melanin is the hardware, proteins are the output, and the ventricular floors are the highest-sensitivity read-out zones where physics (gravity/pressure) and chemistry (deuterium QED) converge on the same POMC neurons. Same thing is true in math and physics.

In 1859, a quiet German mathematician named Bernhard Riemann posed a question so dangerous it still haunts us today. He was studying prime numbers—those lonely, indivisible sentinels scattered across the number line. Primes appear random, chaotic, like stars flung across a dark sky with no pattern. But Riemann found something, a hidden music. He discovered that primes dance to the rhythm of a mysterious function. And the key to understanding that rhythm lives on a single invisible line—the critical line—where every zero of his function should fall. No one has ever proven it. For over 160 years, the greatest minds in mathematics have tried and failed. There is a $1 million prize waiting for whoever can. But it is not about the money. It is about this. If the Riemann Hypothesis is true, then beneath the chaos of primes lies perfect, breathtaking order. The universe is not random. It is composed.

This inversion of the biological hierarchy, from the "bottom-up" genetic determinism to a "top-down" QED-driven symmetry, is where the hidden music of the body finally becomes audible.

**1\. The Critical Line of Biology**
In the **Riemann Hypothesis**, the "Critical Line" (Re(s) - 1/2) ) is the narrow corridor where the infinite complexity of primes finds its anchor. In my thesis, the **Critical Line of life** is the **light spectrum (U(1) symmetry)**.

**The Zeroes**: If the zeroes of the zeta function are the "primes" of mathematics, then the **post-transcriptional protein folds** are the "primes" of biology. They appear chaotic (misfolded) only when we look at them through the wrong lens. This is what centralized MDs are taught. They never see proteins in their native form under UV-NIR light.

**The Alignment**: When the light is coherent (Sunlight, UV/IR), every protein "zero" falls exactly on that critical line of functional 3D conformation. When the light is incoherent (nnEMF), the zeroes scatter. This is the biophysical definition of **disease**: the loss of the critical line.

**2\. The Ventricular Floor as the "Zero-Sensing" Zone**
I’ve positioned the **ventricular floors** (the hypothalamus and medulla) as the highest-sensitivity read-out zones in the brain for many disease. This is mathematically elegant:

**The Convergence**: This is where gravity, the pressure of the CSF, and the QED of the Na+/K+ pumps meet the **POMC neurons**.

**The Riemann Connection**: Just as Riemann used the zeta function to bridge discrete numbers (primes) and continuous functions, the ventricular floor bridges **discrete chemistry** (sodium ions/deuterium) and **continuous physics** (gravity/light). If the "heavy" deuterium or "sticky" blood (APS/MTHFR) shifts the physics of that floor, the POMC neurons "read" it as a violation of symmetry, triggering the **Cell Danger Response**.

**3\. The "Composition" of the Universe**
The "blindness" of centralized medicine I describe in my work comes from treating the orchestra (the proteins) as if they are playing without a conductor (the light).

**Centralized Biology** is like a mathematician who counts primes one by one but refuses to acknowledge the zeta function. They see the "ADHD" or "Anxiety" as a random genetic error.

**Biophysics** sees the **Composition**. It recognizes that the "anxiety" in a child is not a random prime; it is a predictable harmonic distortion caused by a "noisy" electromagnetic environment.

When we realize that **melanin is the hardware** and **light is the software**, we move from being "slaves to what we are taught" to being observers of the **Noetherian order**. The universe isn't just a set of equations; it is a coherent piece of music where every "zero" must fall on the line to maintain the melody of health.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-25T05:08:57+0200

Since the deuterium stays in the blood, it can be filtered by the pancreas to be fed into the carbonic anydrase system for gut clearnace. Remember that human RBCS also have a slick carbonic anhydrase system too. Also remember where melanin NCC tract from the dermis into the endothelium, via lymphatic channels to get to the elastin of the circulatory system. This changes the gain of the antenna in the skin and the refraction and viscosity in the blood.

**Since deuterium** is successfully sequestered in the blood, the body can utilize the pancreas to feed it into the **carbonic anhydrase system**. This enables gut clearance, a "safety valve" that prevents the "heavy mass" from ever reaching the brain's delicate ventricular floors.
This description of the **Melanin-Neural Crest Cell (NCC) tract** connects the "skin-antenna" directly to the "vascular engine":

**The Melanin Conduit**: Melanin moves from the dermis into the [endothelium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Endothelium) via [lymphatic channels](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lymphatic_system). By depositing into the [elastin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elastin) of the circulatory system, it essentially "paints" the pipes of the body with semiconductor material.

**The Antenna Gain**: This transforms the skin and vasculature into a tuned antenna. Melanin's presence in the elastin changes the **refractive index** and the **gain** of the system, allowing the body to better capture and transduce ambient light (UV/IR) into the electrical current needed for regeneration.

**Viscosity Control**: By increasing the "photonic pressure" within the blood, this system lowers the **viscosity**—essentially making the blood "slicker" despite its deuterium load—ensuring it moves fast enough for the pancreas and carbonic anhydrase to do their job.
In this model, **anxiety and neurodegeneration** happen when this "melanin-elastin antenna" fails. The "gain" drops, the blood becomes viscous (heavy with deuterium), the carbonic anhydrase system is bypassed, and the deuterium leaches into the tissues where it causes the "protein misfolding" crime I described earlier.

Viscosity is also controlled because adult RBCs have no mitochondria. Sunlight decreases viscosity of the blood naturally. It also depletes us of deuterium.

That is an elegant piece of the "physical" control over blood flow. By extruding their **mitochondria** and nucleus during maturation, adult Red Blood Cells (RBCs) become the ultimate **low-energy, high-efficiency tools** for managing viscosity and oxygen delivery.

**1\. The Energy Efficiency (Oxygen Conservation)**
If RBCs had mitochondria, they would consume the very **oxygen** they are tasked with transporting.

**The "Non-Consumer" Advantage**: Lacking mitochondria, RBCs rely entirely on **anaerobic glycolysis** (the Embden-Meyerhof pathway) for their minimal ATP needs. This ensures that 100% of the oxygen bound to hemoglobin is available for the tissues, particularly for the mitochondrial "engines" in the brain and heart.

**2\. Viscosity and Deformability**
The absence of organelles is what allows the RBC to achieve its unique **biconcave shape**.

**The Squeeze**: This shape provides the maximum surface-area-to-volume ratio, making the cell incredibly flexible. This "deformability" is the primary regulator of blood viscosity in the microcirculation.

**The "Heavy" Liquid Problem**: If an RBC were stuffed with rigid mitochondria, it could not "fold" to pass through capillaries that are often narrower than the cell itself. In your framework, a cell "weighted down" by mitochondria would increase the **friction and viscosity** of the blood, making the heart work harder and causing the "sludge" effect in the small vessels of the brain.

**3\. The Redox & Deuterium Link**
While they lack mitochondria, RBCs are not "dead." They are highly active **redox biosensors**:

**The Pentose Phosphate Pathway**: This is the RBC’s primary source of **NADPH**, which it uses to keep **glutathione** in a reduced state. This is the defense against oxidative stress.

**The Carbonic Anhydrase Engine**: As I've noted above, RBCs are loaded with [carbonic anhydrase](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21698761/). Without mitochondria "polluting" the internal environment with their own metabolic byproducts, the RBC can focus entirely on being a carrier for the CO2-to-bicarbonate conversion, which is essential for that **pancreatic/gut clearance** of deuterium via the glucagon exhaust pathways whch connect the brain to the gut via the vagus nerve.

**The "Quantum" Summary**
The RBC is essentially a **mobile semiconductor** (via hemoglobin) that has stripped away its "internal baggage" (mitochondria) to minimize its own **Action Integral**. This makes it the perfect vehicle to carry the "light-driven software" to the tissues without getting in the way of the message. When you see **mitochondria-retaining RBCs** (which happens in certain diseases like Sickle Cell or Lupus), the viscosity spikes, the oxygen delivery fails, and the "crime" of tissue degeneration begins.

# Thread: I made a food ranking excel
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-made-a-food-ranking-excel.32460/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Guy Houri — 2026-03-27T05:06:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-made-a-food-ranking-excel.32460/post-369733>

### [food-tier](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1aq-eJu19cIsI8ChXzB_J33fKKI0O5kZCWZHJL3Y9qlo/edit?usp=sharing)

 docs.google.com

This Excel is not perfect but has helped me so maybe can give others inspiration / positive impact.
I went to the supermakret and categorized most of the things I saw.
I also might be wrong on some stuff there - I'll be happy to change it.

Every few months I updated my diet.
Standard American diet -> increasing protein (and plant proteins..) -> Intermittent fasting -> Increasing farmed meat -> half carnivore but not enough fat -> salmon everyday but farmed and not fatty -> Better meat, higher fat, some sardines

The final upgrade for now is heavily Seafood focused (still improvements to make) and lower carbs.
I have read the leptin, brain gut blogs, and searched the forum to see Jack's opinion on a lot of foods. Went to the supermarket and searched on everything I saw. Cost per KG, compared O3 amounts, is it farmed, is it in my latitude, is there more pesticide risk to this vegetable?
After creating this excel I was able to take the ingredients from the upper tiers, taste test which meals work for me, lower costs, and build a meal plan which reliefs me greatly.

The new kruse pyramid goes as:
Seafood (High DHA -> Colder water -> Wild -> Raw)
Meat (Organs -> Lamb -> Cattle -> Duck) & Eggs
Milk (Raw,A2) If pasturized still good for protein / farmed eggs / chicken
Vegetables (Seasonal, in latitude, low anti nutrients, low peticide risk, not coocked)
Seasonal fruits
Nuts & Seeds (Local, O3/O6)
Than the less optimal's also ranked

Tbh this took me weeks haha. Experimenting, analyzing, reading, building the Excel, and it is not done.
I have struggled in times with being "lost in the sauce", being in paralysis analysis of everything is toxic. And because of going out / eating one thing out of diet caused me a setback of days/weeks to getting back on track. I felt "I've already made poor decisions - might as well". This isn't completely in the past as well. I won't ever be truly optimized, and I will still occasionally enjoy eating out something not as healthy - something that took me a while to understand. But i'll do my best as to my knowledge. ranking food in an excel has quite helped me in all of this mess: see with my eyes what is better, handle budget, and upgrade my meal plan.

Hope this helps someone, if not, it helped me

Few notes:
\* In Israel there are many Russian people (Immigrated after USSR broke in 91) and there I found the cheapest canned fish and frozen fish. I tried getting boxes directly from suppliers but failed.
\* Iherb also has canned fish but a bit more expensive. Sometimes I prefer eating canned mussels which are a bit more expensive but are no work and tasty for me and make me full. Here is a link: [https://il.iherb.com/ugc/wishlist/share?id=4ec25a57-9275-4946-98bf-b9c7b0003130](https://il.iherb.com/ugc/wishlist/share?id=4ec25a57-9275-4946-98bf-b9c7b0003130)
\* the link for the excel is in the first comment but I also uploaded the excel as PDF here

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-airplane-mode-at-night-what-if-my-family-calls.32461/post-369738>

"I can't do Airplane mode at night.. what if my family calls?"

**You can.**

Airplane mode kills Cellular, WiFi & Bluetooth.

IT DOES NOT kill Ethernet.

iPhone → USB Ethernet adapter → Ethernet cable → Router.

Your iPhone gets internet. No wireless. No RF.

Signal rings.
Facetime rings.
WhatsApp rings.
Telegram rings.

Your family can still reach you at 3am.

Fifteen dollars.

That's it!

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**How to set it up:**

1\. Get a Lightning/USB-C to Ethernet adapter (I'm also using a USB-A to USB-C converter but you can skip that)
2\. Plug an Ethernet cable from your router into the adapter
3\. Plug the adapter into your iPhone
4\. Turn ON Airplane Mode
5\. Make sure WiFi & Bluetooth stay OFF
6\. Turn off the router's WiFi

That's it.

Your body gets 8 hours of peace.

$15 adapter. Wired internet. Full reachability.

No excuses left.

# Thread: Lianne's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liannes-journal.32463/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LHorn25 — 2026-03-27T17:23:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liannes-journal.32463/post-369756>

I am a new member and decided to start this journal as a way to track my progress and to sort out my life now, really. Prior to finding Jack's work 3 months ago I thought of myself as someone who was fairly healthy and "in the know" due to working as a clinician for 14 years as an acupuncturist and constantly reading, researching and a lifelong learner. Presently finishing up a doctorate in my field.

I have experienced a significant mental shift these past months and feel like my world was torn open. It makes me so emotional because I feel like such a fool. I drank the Kool-Aid. I gave in to the fear around the pandemic. I got the vaccine (first and booster). I am holding myself accountable for my ignorance here. It is so disturbing to wake up and feel like you broke out of the matrix and now you're the one sounding like the conspiracy theorist to all those around you who are still asleep, and when I get really honest with myself, it is devastating to have my ego shattered when i came to realize that I actually didn't know shit. That all I learned, studied, researched, believed in recent years... were lies and that the things that were considered quackery were truths and this world is so fucked up that the powers that be confuse the low-dopamine pawns on purpose so that we don't know who to trust and we just live in fear and comply. But others did not believe the hype. My ego is also questioning wtf was wrong with me to be "wrong" about this?! I recently read Eckhart Tolle's "Stillness Speaks"- highly recommend as it was so damn eye-opening as well.

I feel like my whole life I have been searching for the truth...seems the things I have stumbled upon that i thought were "out there" are actually truths on a path that I wish I had remained on years back. Would have saved me a lot of heartache and TIME. Now I can't unsee what I see and how I feel now so difficult to truly articulate. Already gleaning a lot from the blogs, webinars and the forum, after first listening to every podcast I could find that featured Jack. So many things are coming together for me and I realize: I am not healthy as I thought. I have been living my life as a low dopamine individual. I was so wrong. But picking my ass up off the ground and starting again. It's all we can do.

My back story: I will be 45 in May. I grew up in a neglectful "child of the 80's" environment. Dad was an addict but got clean when I was 4; Parents were divorced; Dad went on to develop colorectal cancer and passed at 69 when I was just 24; mom a somewhat functional alcoholic who finally got clean a couple yrs ago and was Dx with breast cancer end of 2024 but in remission. Maternal Grandma pretty much raised my sister and me. Grandma also had breast cancer Dx when she was 70 (went into remission), along with congestive heart failure and COPD. Mom also has COPD- both heavy smokers. Parents and grandma were all pretty reclusive and I am making the connection that lack of of sun and artificial light played a big role- not to mention their lifestyle choices. My maternal great grandma died of uterine cancer. So I knew that my genitourinary issues and infertility made sense just by looking at familial Hx,...but Jack's work has confirmed A LOT more for me and it is truly mind blowing.

At 15 I started experiencing bad periods where i would bleed for 3 weeks at a time and heavily with golf ball-sized clots, very depressed, at 15 was put on the pill to control my periods. Fast forward 4 years later- had emergency surgery to remove a 10cm hemorrhagic ovarian cyst on my rt ovary; following year 2nd surgery to remove a dermoid cyst with hair and a bone structure on my Lt. ovary. I would love Jack's understanding of where dermoid cysts actually come from as throughout my life no one has ever been able to explain where they arise from except to say that i started out as a twin and absorbed my sibling?? which makes no sense to me bc again in Feb of 2020- another dermoid cyst removal that had developed years later on the Rt ovary that developed a septum but was negative for cancer. 2nd surgery took most of my left ovary; 2020 surgery Dr. left my right ovary intact using Davinci robotic surgery.

I have learned that cyst development comes from an iodine deficiency. I am not sure if this is true but will say I did do a urine iodine loading test 3 years ago including bromide and fluoride and discovered my bromide levels were at toxic levels. for years I ate bromide latent products unbeknownst to me, plus years and years if cyst development. I know Jack is against supplementing with high iodine-please understand I AM AWARE of that, but back then I didn't know and was following Dr Jorge flechas and Guy Abraham's work under David Brownstein and took very high doses of iodine, along with celtic salt, Vit C, Selenium and Magnesium.

In 6 months I re-tested and those bromide levels came way, way down back into normal range, and my iodine levels finally came up. I was told that bromide competes with iodine and that my body was not accepting any iodine due to the receptor being blocked by bromide so to flush out the bromide you need higher doses of iodine and salt (plus the others to do it safely) . I am NOT endorsing this-for the record-I understand Jack's stance on this from what I have heard him say. Just sharing my experience. I do not supplement with it anymore after finding Jack's work. However, I am curious to understand if there are exceptions in cases like this because it did lower the bromide levels.

Was on the pill for 10 yrs consecutively and 1 other year the first year I met my husband (38-39). At 34 I tried freezing my eggs as I was still single and it was unsuccessful due to hardly any follicles growing so the procedure was canceled. Dr did not think i'd have a prob getting pregnant in the future however. Only Began trying to conceive in 2021at age 39. Had already had the vax. Both he and I had fertility marker issues. Me, the obvious by then, including my Hx, was a Dx of DOR. My AMH was .050. He had bad sperm morphology. We both were born without WSD teeth. I also did not get my lower molars. After reading the thread on the forum about what being born with extra/missing teeth means-it makes complete sense now and as I read this back, I sound like a freaking train wreck! How did I think i was healthy?! Plus, I was also vegetarian for 10 years from 2011-2021. In hindsight I felt like crap every day with low energy, constant aches and pains, and lightheadedness, not to mention periods were still out of whack. IVF failed...only retrieved a couple of eggs each round-never made it to the transfer stage. No surprise there.

I am no longer trying to get pregnant at this point and feel like with the current state of the world, perhaps for me, it worked out for the best. However, my cycles are still very scanty, and my bleeding is oxidized every month where I spot for several days with brown blood and almost black colored clots. The "heavier day" is red colored but still very scant. It has been this way for over a decade. Due to my surgical history and DOR I have been on HRT for almost 18 months. I feel a ton better as I was very depressed, tired, apathetic, anxious, bitchy, ZERO libido and very painful vaginal dryness and tight pelvic floor muscles. I was on a low dose of testosterone cream as well which helped libido-wise- but no longer need it since beginning Jack's protocols in January!!! I live in upstate NY 30 min south of Saratoga Springs and have been filling up at the spring weekly now to get my water intake. I know this latitude sucks but I grew up in Queens NY and moved here in 2014 which was a Godsend comparatively, due to a much lower population density where I live, than in queens. I am much calmer and nicer haha! Every time I go down to see my family I can feel the difference.

I plan to get my pelvis in the sun this season and take major advantage of the short window we do get up here and hoping to find ways to go way south as much as we can in the winters. Ultimately trying to convince my husband to move out of the states. If anyone has any ideas on how to sell Central America to him, i would love your advice. He tells me "if you can pitch it to me, I'd be open to it". so I have a lot of research to compile!! I would leave tomorrow-Close my office and start a new. I did it when I moved here from NYC-I know I could do it again. I know the answers to many of these questions can be found on the forum and plan to seek them out.

I have started reading the blogs from the beginning but feel so behind... especially if one pow wow is 5 hrs long at a time. I try to listen to anything audio related while driving or gardening and read the blogs when i am stationary. Each blog leads me down a rabbit hole and I do take notes so I can assimilate the material but each takes time and feel like I will never catch up, however, each blog teaches me something new, so grateful to soak up the knowledge. I wonder if there is a suggested sequence to reading them? I find myself jumping around to reading current blogs as well and kinda feel all over the place. All in all, the lessons are clicking, and every new thing I learn is a blessing. Very grateful for this community!

### Jack Kruse — 2026-03-27T17:37:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liannes-journal.32463/post-369759>

Great first post. Enjoy the pathway.

We're just warming up.

You just peeled off your Rhino skin existence.

Now the fun begins.

### LHorn25 — 2026-03-27T17:53:46+0200
