# Thread: Cell phone radiation the next centralized "health craze"?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-phone-radiation-the-next-centralized-health-craze.32520/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### stuart31 — 2026-04-08T05:15:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-phone-radiation-the-next-centralized-health-craze.32520/post-370460>

Is cell phone radiation the next “health craze” for people who follow centralized advice?
Intentionally spending time in cities with high exposure to cell towers or strapping multiple bluetooth devices to your head to “EMF proof” you from cancer…

From the Darkhorse podcast a few weeks back I came across this X post regarding a research paper showing lab mice exposed to cell phone radiation lived longer and were healthier.

[https://x.com/zanehkoch/status/2033400775762964601](https://x.com/zanehkoch/status/2033400775762964601)

He hypothesizes that the cell radiation could be giving a hormetic effect thus preventing tumour growth. I was flabbergasted at first by the evidence but Bret, an evolutionary biologist, explains the results are actually counterintuitive as to what is really going on.

In an unusual phenomena due to selective breeding, these mice under normal conditions only live to be about 8-9 months old before dying from lymphocytic leukemia. From being bred very early on in life, these mice exhibit properties of having no resistance to tumours and very fast tissue regeneration. Typically they will show signs of disease within 8 weeks of life and start having health issues at 4 months. The same non-laboratory mice would usually live up to 1.5 to 3 years.

The effect explained by Weinstein is that by exposing the mice to something equivalent to a toxin (cell phone radiation) at a level that would be less than the amount required to kill them, it would paradoxically make them live longer because the toxin would slow down the rapidly growing cancer cells and have a less profound effect on healthy tissue.

Long story short: cell phone radiation is good for lab mice, but bad for humans as it clearly has a negative effect on cell growth.

It's kind of scary to think of the implications for this kind of research. Essentially with these mice they can produce research saying something that is essentially poison actually promotes longevity.

[https://www.youtube.com/live/ZoZIte3T0Ec?si=m9-tsKpy17-Pkbku](https://www.youtube.com/live/ZoZIte3T0Ec?si=m9-tsKpy17-Pkbku)

They start talking about the study at 27:40

### Johan Lindøm — 2026-04-08T10:52:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cell-phone-radiation-the-next-centralized-health-craze.32520/post-370469>

What does melanin do? Does it convert broad‑spectrum frequencies into usable energy — including cosmic and non‑native EMFs — and thus act as a protective layer that early life used to survive harsh environments? Are non‑native frequencies natural, and do they deplete melanin faster or convert it into something detrimental like lipofuscin? If so, what are the mechanisms and consequences? What do modern humans need more of?

In The Invisible Rainbow, Arthur Firstenberg explores the "counterintuitive" you mention. The book review some of the science on this topic.

A father of a friend of my daughter claimed non‑native EMFs are beneficial and will extend life, citing mainstream science — an example of how half‑truths and faulty reasoning can become full lies.

Some provocative questions!

Yes I suppose that is true how POMC/melanin being a wide band gapped semiconductor can take a wider range of frequencies including nnEMF and convert them into more melanin and regenerative currents. The last blog tells how even people in Chernobyl can turn deadly levels of radiation into melanin on their surfaces. The origins of life were under the full spectrum radiation from the sun including “harmful” UVC.

The simplest way to answer without getting into specifics of different frequencies of 1-5g, quantum effects and electron spin which I'm still trying to wrap my head around. I'd say that nnEMF's diminish the EZ water required to store that usable energy produced by melanin.

Even if someone was in full sun doing all the “right things” but still getting hammered by nnEMF they **would** be getting beneficial infrared light to make ATP and metabolic water but are simultaneously dehydrating semiconductive surfaces and draining their cellular "batteries" via less EZ water. The result is dehyrdated tissues and deuterium being in places it shouldn't resulting in low redox potential, failing mitochondria and ultimately rising heteroplasmy. Certainly there's more negative effects than just that..

Thought experiment: Would it be possible to make a cell phone tower that would **increase** EZ water? Probably not but it's interesting to think about!

# Thread: My Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.32521/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dee Dee — 2026-04-08T11:49:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.32521/post-370472>

Grew up stable home, parents together, always out on the lake surfing behind the boat and lots of beautiful fresh seafood. Started eating oysters as a baby

60 y.o. Aussie woman, non- smoker; mum of 4 (including twins) and proud nan of 2 little boys.

Caesarean birth, bottle fed, mother smoked in pregnancy- born one month early.

Migraine and colic / IBS (from childhood); German Measles; Glandular Fever (16); Episodes of back pain (Fibrositis at 19); sinus issues.

Always hard to wake up mornings. Sleepwalker, sleeptalker, acting out dreams (incident recently).

MVA- concussion and whiplash (23); started daily headache; ongoing neck pain then depression and deconditioning.

Tetany (24)

Births two natural (27; 32) and one caesarean (twins) (29); mild sciatica; severe lower back pain episodes.

Spondylolisthesis -rolled over on the lounge (L5/ S1) (40). Tried nerve blocks, medications, therapies and eventually opted for a fusion (L5/S1) (at 43). Helped sciatica but more pain in low back and legs. 3 Foramotomies for scar tissue, spurs etc causing weakness and pain and spinal cyst.

Sleep disorder (not enough REM sleep- told to nap- helps).

Diplopia (Double Vision Cranial Nerve V! (virus or blood clot on my brain). Horrendous headache and eye pain. Diplopia lasted 3 months and left with chronic migraine and pain behind both eyes. Chronic Fatigue Syndrome and Fibromyalgia diagnosed 2009 (at 44)- extremely ill with flu like symptoms, sweating, sore glands, fatigue. For many years I couldn’t read (voracious reader).

Was a single mum raising 4 kids and studying Psych by distance. Graduated 4th year 2018. Unable to work. Extreme stress in my marriage- husband abusive. Loved being a mum.

Perimenopause symptoms rough especially sweating. Menopause really rough- sweating, hot flushes, insomnia, extreme depression, muscle pain (worse than normal), joint pain (I dont usually have). Irritability and anger and felt nuts. Tried different things. HRT has given me my life back.
Previous endometriosis (good now).

Craved and ate oysters in my pregnancies. I lived an outdoors life with my children, swimming and bbq’s at the beach or lake.

Been spending long hours at the beach every day.

Currently -severe thoracic pain (physio helping). Lost 14 kg so far (Endep caused weight gain).

Cheers everyone,
Donna

I'm provifin you with a profound mechanical inversion of how we view "disease." If the heart is a **vortex engine** rather than a pressure pump, an MI isn't just a "clogged pipe", it is a **phase-lock failure** in a gravity well.

**1\. The Carrington Event (1859) as the "Initial Global Reset"**

The Carrington Event was a massive solar coronal mass ejection (CME) that literally set telegraph wires on fire.

**The Atmospheric "Charge":** It dumped an unfathomable amount of plasma into the ionosphere, permanently altering the **Earth’s "Background Hum"** (the Schumann Resonance).

**The Lag Effect:** Biological systems don't fail instantly; they "drift." If the Carrington Event "warped" the local magnetic declination, the human **Lattice** began to decouple from the Earth’s natural frequency. By the early 20th century, as industrialization (AC power) added more "noise," the heart’s internal GPS lost its lock.
**2\. MI as a "Magnetic Stall"**

In my framework, the heart is a **Z-axis actuator**. It creates a toroidal field that allows blood to "spiral" without friction.

**The Gravity Well:** If the magnetic environment is "noisy" or "declined" away from the human optimal, the **Vortex** collapses.

**The Stall:** An MI is the moment the "Liquid Crystal" blood stops spiraling and starts "tumbling." This creates the **Inertial Lock** (the clot/blockage). The "stall" happens because the heart can no longer maintain the **Gravity Well** necessary to pull blood through the system.

**3\. Is the Sun "Defibrillating" the Earth?**

This is a radical, hopeful take on what solar flares do for planetary anodes dynamo's.

**The Solar Shock:** If the Earth’s magnetic field is "stagnant" or "Deuterium-heavy" (due to 5G, satellite "shells," and industrial waste), it becomes a **damped oscillator**.

**The CME as a Paddle:** A solar flare is a high-energy **Magnetic Pulse**. It’s the Sun hitting the "Reset" button on the Earth’s ionosphere. It is trying to **kickstart the planetary "Heart"** by clearing the isotopic sludge from the atmosphere.

**4\. The 20th Century "Heart Attack" Explosion**

Why did MI's humans spike after this event?

**The Great Dissonance:** We moved from a DC-world (Natural/Sun-led) to an AC-world (Artificial/Tesla-led).

**Isotopic Buildup:** We began "jamming" our own internal magnetic declination with radio waves and heavy isotopes in the food/water supply.

**The Result:** The human heart, unable to find the "True North" of the Earth’s field amidst the electronic smog, simply **stalls out magnetically**.

An MI is a **Spatial Disorientation of the Blood**. The Sun isn't our enemy; its "flares" are periodic attempts to **Degauss the Planet** and restore the proper magnetic "pitch" so our biological vortexes can spin freely again.

**I think most the "Sudden Cardiac Events" we see today are the result of our internal "Lattice" being unable to handle the Sun's current "Defibrillation" attempts because we are too "heavily loaded" with Deuterium for many reasons but I think the new magnetic decline map will link the highest risk places on Earth with MI's. We should really look at the places where the magnetic field failed the most since 1859 and then superimpose the cardiac morbidity and mortality data with and see what it reveals.
The hypothesis that "Sudden Cardiac Events" correlate with high-latitude magnetic field failures aligns with emerging research in Heliobiology (Decentralized Medicine). While conventional medicine focuses on diet and exercise, a "Magnetic Decline Map" reveals a geographic pattern of cardiac risk that traditional metrics often miss.
5\. The High-Latitude Mortality Spike**

The data shows a clear geographic pattern: the incidence of **Myocardial Infarction (MI)** and hypertension is significantly higher in regions at **higher latitudes**.

**The Horizontal Protection:** The horizontal component of the Earth's magnetic field (hGMF) is strongest at the equator and acts as a "protective factor".

**The Polar Weakness:** As you move toward the poles, the hGMF diminishes, and the intensity of total magnetic field fluctuations (tGMF) increases. These high-latitude zones are where the "Lattice" is most exposed to solar-driven **Geomagnetic Disturbances (GMD)
6\. The South Atlantic Anomaly (SAA)**

One of the most critical "failed" regions is the South Atlantic Anomaly which is a vast area over South America an the Atlantic that now extends to the tip of South Africa.In side the SAA the magnetic field is 50% weaker than elsewhere. This means it acts as a proxy to teachus how it would affect the Human GPS system.

****Radiation Descent = nnEMF vcoming closer to humans.** In this "weak spot," the Van Allen radiation belt descends closer to Earth, exposing the population to higher levels of ionizing radiation and magnetic volatility.

**Cardiac Correlate:** Studies in Brazil have already noted that **women and older adults** in these magnetically disturbed zones show up to **three times the frequency** of MI cases during solar disturbances.

7\. Impact of Geomagnetic Disturbances (GMD)

Since the Carrington Event, the Earth's baseline field has been in a state of flux. Modern studies in 263 U.S. cities confirm that short-term GMDs are strongly associated with daily MI and CVD deaths. (Cite 1)

The "Defibrillation" Effect: A solar storm creates a geoelectric field on Earth. If the human body's "vortex engine" is overloaded with Deuterium (increasing the viscosity and "stickiness" of the blood lattice), it cannot handle the sudden voltage spike.

# Thread: Andrew and Kelly's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrew-and-kellys-optimal-journal.32530/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Andrew.S — 2026-04-10T03:52:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrew-and-kellys-optimal-journal.32530/post-370595>

This is the start of Andrew and Kelly’s optimal journal–we’ve been together as a couple for the past 2 years and just moved in together. Our lifestyles are so intermingled we figured we’d do it together– also because Kelly has some stuff going on (she is not an active member on the site), and its a big impetus (as you’ll read) for my desire (and hers) to work on incorporating the protocols from Jack. She is willing and learning though!! Anyways, here's our story, situation and current POA…

### Andrew.S — 2026-04-10T03:53:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrew-and-kellys-optimal-journal.32530/post-370597>

Andrew (myself)
History
\-born and raised, and currently living in san diego, ca, currently 34 yo
\-active childhood, played a lot of sports, really liked being outside and active
\-fortunately had a mom who instilled “healthy” habits so I’ve always been interested in doing “healthy” things and wanted to be working in the “health and wellness” space
\-had lazy eye as a child, and had the corrective surgery for it when I was a child (4 yo), after which I wore glasses (transition glasses unfortunately) until age 15, which is when I switched to contacts, but ended up not wearing them because they would fly out playing high school football, and I never noticed a difference with them on/off so I stopped wearing them to this very day and see perfectly fine
\-did not have any mentionable health issues growing up, I was a pretty healthy kid
\-i went through a full vaccination schedule as a child (haven’t had one since age 17 including no covid vaxs)
\-I haven’t been sick since high school outside of two instances catching the covid sickness, otherwise I feel like im fairly resilient and high energy
\-wisdom teeth pulled age 17 for 2nd surgery
\-repaired pec tendon (torn bench pressing) age 24 for 3rd surgery, last time I went under.
\-my professional career has mostly been spent as a personal trainer, working inside gyms, but 3 years ago I became a massage therapist and do mostly fascial/body work now, my work is mobile so I go to clients and have the ability to get lots of sun breaks throughout the day.

Currently
\-overall I feel pretty good in terms of my current physical health-no major issues to speak of-primarily interested in continued overall optimization
\-the only issues I deal with are muscular aches/soreness in my body, and occasional gut (Bowel movement) issues (4-6 on bristol stool chart-i never get constipated) which has been on going since mid-20s-I think it’s more mental/emotional stress related (when i got my first grey hairs-but haven’t gotten more since) but soreness/aches is usually resolved though my own body work practices and I've come to learn how to resolve my gut issues with my sunlight protocol since learning jacks work.
\-I started implementing my current sun protocol last sept when I first found Jack's work, and was successful with the gut issues, like I said, but regressed in between daylight savings times with the lack of sunlight, but since then have been good when I get sunshine on my abdomen.
\-spend a lot of time at the beach or at parks in between clients (i have a lot of autonomy with my work schedule and flow) I also have always enjoyed being outside and active so im very motivated to be-its especially easy to do in san diego near the coast.
\-Diet is very consistent with a BAB that includes carbs (from sweet potato), afternoon snack is a tin of sardines, and a protein/fat based dinner.
\-I also like to surf, go out regularly 1-3xs/week (depending on swell and work schedule), and have been an avid cold plunger for 3 or 4 years now. Otherwise, lots of walking, a movement practice, and strength training maybe 1-2x/week.

Recent blood work (done in march):
I took a NMR lipid panel, omega panel, basic metabolic panel, vit D total/individual, CBC, homocysteine,and hscrp. The following are a list of some of the more significant numbers:
Vitamin d: 36ng/ml
Hscrp 0.2
Homocysteine 7
Total Cholesterol 273
HDL 50
LDL 208
Triglycerides 52
Omega 6/3 ratio 4.1
DHA 5.3H
Fasting glucose: 86

My vit D is obviously the biggest red flag (working on it), I used to think LDL was more of a concern but with some of the things I recently learned from the forum/blogs, I’m not as concerned, especially once we move to a new environment.

### Andrew.S — 2026-04-10T03:54:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andrew-and-kellys-optimal-journal.32530/post-370598>

Kelly
History
\- currently 31 yo, she grew up in New Jersey, active, athlete, outdoors a lot, was also sick a lot as a kid,
\-started wearing contacts in 3rd grade, been wearing them ever since
\-had anxiety attacks and anemia in 2nd grade
\-had tonsils removed at 6 years old
\-all wisdom teeth are gone
\-had braces growing up, teeth are still crowded years later
\-in middle/high school, had a lot of gastrointestinal issues
\-had multiple retinal holes in both eyes at age 16 and had laser surgery to repair them (no symptoms - detected via an annual eye exam)
\-had the full vaccine schedule as a kid (no vax since 19 including covid)
\-got diagnosed with celiac when she was 20 (2013)
\-lost her period from 2016-2019 and wrecked her hormones trying to get into body building, this also led to cystic acne on her face which continues to today
\-has had constipation since college, which has actually gone away over the past two months
\-stopped body building and got her period back after working with a functional medicine doctor from 2019-2020 but still has the cystic acne
\-when she was 26 she moved to Scottsdale, AZ
\-first few months in AZ is where she started having debilitating menstrual cycles where she’d be in so much pain she couldn't open up her eyes, as the years went on she started having vomiting and diarrhea on day 1 of her cycle, happens on and off for past 6 years
\-when she was 28 she moved to san diego
\-Known family history: (all on father’s side) grandfather had T2 diabetes, grandmother had cataracts, father has CV disease, triple bypass surgery, father had retinal tears in both eyes in April 2025

Currently
\-she currently works remotely, virtual 1-1 work, spends a lot of time on the computer for work
\-5 months ago (Nov 2025) she noticed some small breast pain in the bottom of her left breast, where there was tenderness and a small lump
\-she also has been waking up between 1-4am with intense nausea and panic attacks, happens in luteal phase mainly
\-breast pain occurs more in ovulation phase
\-over the past 5 months that pain has spread into both breasts, her breast size has increased by 2 cups sizes since then, gained 8 lbs since then (she's a small woman at 5ft tall), she feels extremely inflamed
\-eats a BAB, fish in afternoon, and high protein/high fat dinner; predominantly organic/local
\-use reverse osmosis water that is remineralized via pristine hydro water system
\-wear yellow lens while on computer except for while doing 1-1 work (colored lens mess with ability to do 1-1 sessions)
\-in the process of getting rid of contacts and starting to wear glasses more often
\-One bright note is last week she accidentally ate some gluten (bulgar wheat in tabouli), and had no reaction

Main Blood work (done in Feb)
Kelly took a standard lipid panel, omega panel, basic metabolic panel, vit D total/individual, CBC,and hscrp
Some of her notable numbers are:
HSCRP: 1.0
total Vit D: 29ng/ml
Total Cholesterol 191
HDL 64
LDL 113
Triglycerides 47
Omega 6/3 ratio 8.9
DHA 3.5
Fasting glucose 82

Kelly’s HSCRP, vit D are our current biggest concerns (obvious). This combined with her current symptoms has really got us moving towards taking action on relocating sooner than later.

She has not gone to a doctor yet in regards to the breast issue, a friend (who's a nurse) rec’d a mammogram, but after I studied the forum, I suggested not to, and she’s on board with not doing it. There is a location here that does QT imaging, which apparently is “non-radiation breast scan” but 1: we don’t know if doing the scan (assuming it’s safe) will tell us anything we might not already know, and 2: if it’s worth spending $3500 on to do, when that $ could just be used to relocate– which would be the obvious move if it confirms what we might think it is. Regardless, with her other issues (autoimmune condition) the move was already on the table.

Altered homocysteine levels are a warning shot for areas on Earth that are dangerous to mammals for the Z axis.

Elevated homocysteine levels are a **Biophysical Rosetta Stone** for cardiovascular markers. I use them when i am looking at risk factors for atraumatic splenic ruptures. The spleen turns over RBCs every 150 days, and this explands when the human is pregnant because women have to raise their RBC mass to support the pregnancy while they also hemodilute their blood by keeping more water in the blood. This happens because pregnancy requires a high dielectric constant to grow trilllions of cells from a sperm and egg. The issue with this is the RBC and water mass puts huge pressure on the spleen and if the blood is loaded with deuterium the spleen can rupture just from the atomic mass is has to handle. This is why I look at **Homocysteine** through the our Human "Vortex" lens in my patients, because honocysteine stops being a "methylation marker" and becomes a **Strain Gauge** for the Z-axis. (heart due to deuterium excess). The arrows mark the splenic rupture. Almost anyone who has a spleen rupture always has PAD somewhere in their system and their hearts are more susceptible to rhythm difficulties. This is why homocysteine is yoked to MI risk = loss of magentic flux.

Homocysteine levels rise precisely when the **Dielectric Constant of the Water Lattice (k)** collapses from **160 down to 78**, usually in response to the **Magnetic Declination I**’ve identified. That is you partners great risk now.

**1\. Homocysteine as a "Sulfur-Charge" Buffer**

Homocysteine is a sulfur-containing amino acid. Sulfur is a "Heavy" element that is critical for **Electron Transfer that I talked about in the melanin blogs in reference to ferrodoxin biology.

The Vortex Function:** In a high-flux, high-vortex environment, homocysteine is rapidly converted into **Cysteine** or **Methionine**. This process requires a high **Proton Motive Force** and efficient **Fractionation**. this means you need UV-NIR while grounded to a strong magnetic field. If any of these are weak homocysteine will rise. Most allopathic MDs have no idea homocysteine is an EXCESS deuterium measure in their tissues from their blood.

Centralized medicine has their head up their asses on this. None of them realizes how the blood is vortexed by the heart to push deuterium to the endothelial surface. It is amazing how ignorant these people are. You do not hate centralized medicine enough.

I'm exposing the "Big Pharma" shell game here folks. By focusing on ApoB and Lp(a) as "villains" to be drugged, they are treating the bandage as the cause of the wound. In my decentralized model, these aren't markers of a "bad diet", they are Dielectric Patch Kits sent to a vessel that is losing its Z-axis charge from the vortexing done by a 4 chambered heart losing its matrix power.

**2\. The "Mitochondrial Flare" (UPEs to nDNA)** The mitochondria are the Rulers of the Lattice. When they stall due to nnEMF or Deuterium loading, they can't communicate via the 9,000 RPM magnetic spin anymore.
The Distress Signal: Instead, they emit Ultra-weak Photon Emissions (UPEs). These are high-energy "flares" signaling mitochondrial "fire" (oxidative stress).
The Genetic Trigger: These UPEs hit the nucleus (nDNA) and act as a Photonic Switch, upregulating the production of ApoB and Lp(a). The nucleus isn't "broken"; it is responding to the light-signal that the power plant is failing.

**3\. ApoB/Lp(a) as the "Isotopic Sealant"** Why these specific proteins? Because they are designed to manage Interfacial Water.
The Leak: When nnEMF destroys the endothelial melanin and Deuterium leaches into the vessel wall, the dielectric constant () of the blood-water interface collapses from 160 back to 78.
The Repair: Lp(a) is highly "sticky" and contains disulfide bonds that act as a Charge-Capacitor. It is sent to the site of the "dielectric leak" to prevent the vessel from becoming a pro-oxidative swamp.
The Wallet Biopsy: Dr. Attia and "Centralized Science" see the Lp(a) "scab" and want to strip it off with a drug. But without the Lp(a), the vessel will simply short-circuit, leading to the MI "stall" we discussed in my work.

**4\. The "Elastin-Fibrillin" Deuterium Trap This is the "Smoking Gun" for patients:** Your vessel walls (Elastin) are supposed to be flexible springs. Deuterium replaces light hydrogen in these proteins, turning the "spring" into a "brittle stick." This is how PAD begins. The mechanical "Throb" of the heart now causes micro-fractures in the vessel because the "Lattice" is too heavy. The rise in ApoB is the body trying to "Grout"these fractures. If you look at the splenic rupture pics in here everyone of them had aortic PAD in them.

**5\. Reframing for the Patient:** The "Power Plant" Analogy To explain this to someone being "wallet biopsied": The House (Your Body): Your power plant (Mitochondria) is sparking and failing because you have "dirty electricity" (nnEMF) coming in. The Flare (UPEs): The plant sends up a flare to the main office (nDNA). The Repair Crew (ApoB/Lp(a)): The office sends out a repair crew with duct tape and grout to keep the walls from collapsing. Big Pharma's Solution: They want to shoot the repair crew so you don't see them on the security camera (your blood test), while the power plant continues to burn.

**The Z-axis Stall:** When the magnetic field fails (like in the **SAA** or the high latitudes during a decline), the **Lorentz Lift** on the blood vortex disappears. The "9,000 RP second" motor slows, and the system loses its ability to "spin" sulfur into its coherent, functional forms. Homocysteine "pools" in the blood like **Isotopic Sludge**.

**6\. The "Endothelial Sandpaper" Effect**

High homocysteine is toxic to the endothelium.

**My Thesis:** It isn't just "toxic"; it is **Abrasive**. In a low-vortex environment, the blood becomes **Viscous and Turbulent**.

**The Mechanical Damage:** The "Heavy" homocysteine molecules act like sandpaper against the vessel walls. This triggers the **UPE "Flares"** we discussed, which then call in the **ApoB and Lp(a) "Repair Crews."

The Z-axis Warning:** A high homocysteine level is the body's signal that the **X-axis Mastication** and **Z-axis Magnetic Lift** are insufficient to keep the "Lattice" from becoming a pro-oxidative swamp.

**7\. The 0.66 eV Connection**

The conversion of homocysteine requires **B12, Folate, and B6**.

**The Light Link:** These B-vitamins are **Chromophores**. They absorb specific frequencies of light to facilitate electron transitions.

**The Stall:** If the patient is "Light-Starved" (Fitzpatrick 1, living indoors, blocked Meckel's Cave), they can't provide the **0.66 eV Photonic Kick** needed for these enzymes to work. The Homocysteine rise is the "Check Engine" light for **Internal Tanning Failure**.

**8\. Zipcode Diagnostics: The "Dangerous" Earth**

I have proposed in the blogs that Homocysteine is a warning for **"Dangerous Places."

The Mapping:** If you superimpose a Homocysteine map over the **Magnetic Declination Map**, the highest levels will be in the "Stalls" (like the West/Midwest U.S. or the SAA).

**The 51st North Latitude:** This is where the "Lift" is lowest and the D20 loading is highest. But in the Southern Hemisphere that latitude is now 15 south due to the SAA. The rising Homocysteine is the evidence that the **Mammalian Vortex** can no longer handle the **Local Isotopic Viscosity**.

**My Decentralized Summary

Homocysteine is the "Viscosity Meter" of the blood.** It tells you exactly how much "Magnetic Drag" your system is experiencing. If it's high, your **Z-axis Gravity Well** in your heart is failing, and your "Lattice" is being shredded by its own internal friction. ApoB and Lp(a) are "Adaptive response" proteins to a loss of dielectric constant in the blood. They are the biophysical footprint of a system trying to maintain its Dielectric Integrity in a world of declining magnetic flux and rising electronic noise. This is a band gap collapse of elastin on the intima. Centralized MDs and PhDs people should drawn and quartered for posting BS.

It is the final "Warning Shot" before the **Spleen Rupture** or the **MI "Stall"** occurs. Pregnancy always exacerbates this and the baby is likely going to be born with mitochondrial maladaies tied to deuterium inside the matrix leading to many epigenetic and genetic errors.

### CA1010 — 2026-04-11T00:41:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-crisa.32533/post-370678>

Optimal Journal
I began listening to Dr. Kruse and reading his information after my 3rd son was born in 2012. I am a new member here.
I was depleted and fatigued after birthing & nursing 3 boys non-caesarean 2006-2009-2012. My insomnia began in 2006 after my 1st son was born and I still do not get enough deep sleep. Body pain, neuropathy, etc began after several years of not sleeping well. Had 10 amalgams replaced in 2019. Had gut issues (SIBO), tested negative in 2020. Tested positive for mild sleep apnea in 2024. Melatonin cortisol saliva tests showed normal.
I've had a spot on my right leg of vitiligo since I was a child - it's got lots of freckles now so definitely better. I use the sperti red UV lamp on it (in addition to all the sunshine). I use a NIR-A sauna a few times a week to get a good sweat.

I have been getting sun, grounding and implementing all I can since 2012. I was doing much better 2 years ago. Lots of sun. Then one day I drank a Tbsp of MCT oil at bedtime and it was like magic. I did this ongoing and upped my ghee, butter, mct during the day and just continued to get better and actually sleep. My pain disappeared and I felt so good, lots of energy. It lasted about 3 months (during the summer) and then everything came back. I upped the fats - sometimes it helps but not always. Now I'm 46 and have hormonal struggles along with the body pain and insomnia. Especially dryness (skin, eyes, vaginal).
I've have TMD since wisdom teeth removal at 18 years old and I clench at night. I have always been a mouth breather (my parents were heavy smokers and would not open windows ever). I have a few spots on my right lung. I cannot breath out and hold very long - my CO2 tolerance is not good. I have mouth taped for a few years now. I wake up generally around 2-3am in panic and can't go back to sleep for hours most nights for the last 20 years. I have worked in the last 6 months to only nose breathe and keep my tongue on the roof of my mouth. I follow Erwan LeCorre as you suggest but havn't progressed with breath holds.
I took accutane and all the acne antibiotics in my teens, along with birth control for 10 years.
I have always had constipation. It doesn't even get better with high fat. I have never seemed to absorb nutrients from food well.

Height 5'2" current weight 120# (worked the last 2 years to gain some weight as I have always stayed from 100-108# since I reached max height in 9th grade. )
Waist cir 29.5
I was a sprinter. I began running competitively from 10-18 years of age. Athletic build and lean. I still walk several miles every day and do sprints, lift weights - mostly body weight exercises. I do stretching everyday but it never helps pain.
I have some ketones daily. I generally get to a GKI of <2 weekly. My glucose is usually over 100 at wake (assume cortisol issues from not sleeping all night).

Maternal haplotype H1A3
I tend to have very low blood pressure and my glucose is usually avg 90-110 (pretty high considering I eat a few carbs in season but mostly seafood, grass fed beef (we raise in OK on rural land without spray) and animal fats and coconut oil, along with 4-6 egg yolks per day.
Because of Dr Kruse Info, I began eating lots of seafood years ago and try to have sardines (most days), shrimp (2x/wk), salmon (1x/wk) and oysters (canned) several times per week.
I live in the mid-west currently (KS pop 55,000). We are near a Bio lab and a military base. I grew up in rural southern OK (pop <2,000).
I walk at sunrise every morning, get at least a few hours of full sun daily and ground several hours/day. We turn our electrical box off every night. I go to sleep by 9pm and am always very tired at night but wide awake by 2am. We do not use LED lights in the house. We turn off lights at sunset and I wear red glasses when in any light after sunset. (Done this for at least 5 years)
I began going noticeably gray at age 20 and am mostly white/gray now.
I have always had Fitzpatrick type 1 burning easily, red undertone with lots of freckles. I now can be out in the sun all day and not burn. I set up a cover and can tan naked and do most days when the sun is out and temps above 60.
I walk in the lowest temps in KS and the hottest. I do CT (most days in the summer, occasionally in the winter). I can get lots of melanin after a plunge in the summer and laying out in the sun. I learned this from Dr. Kruse and it's amazing how well it works. My melanin is naturally red in color. I must suck melanin inside because I still look light even though I don't burn anymore.
I had been diagnosed with adrenal fatigue in 2012 - when it was still a diagnosis. I have issues with cortisol/adrenal/hypothalamus.

I currently take progesterone (200mg) at bed. It doesn't make me sleepy. I take vaginal estrogen daily. I take an adrenal cortex daily. I use to use DHEA but it has never increased in my labs.
I used DDW from Jan 2026-March2026 (3 month protocol). My cycle restored to 28 days and felt good for a month and a half. Then just started feeling off and in pain again for the last month and half. I don't understand this.

Labs (I have many labs from 2021-2025 Dr's never know how to help me but always suggest more labs & more supplements):
Leptin 2.4 (2024) ; 11.8 (2025) -- had been working here to gain weight
HbA1c 5.6
Glucose 96

Folate 15.2 Vitamin D 69 B12 1490 Vitamin A 70 Vitamin B6 27 Zinc 72
ANA positiveA in 2021 1:80 Nuclear dots A

I would like to get any suggestions on improving deep restorative sleep.
I would like to have more optimal hormones and reduce body pain.
Is it just more time or are there some things I could add to move in the right direction.
I have tried so many supplements and in the last year have stopped after reading all the info from Dr. Kruse.
I work to read all the blogs and listen to all the podcasts. Since starting this membership, I have been going back through all the Q&A. I don't pretend to comprehend most but continue to work to get better at my understanding.

You went gray at 20. Big tell.
The Logic: Your system realized early on (post-wisdom teeth) that the magnetic "Lift" was failing. It "bleached" the hair to lower the Refractive Index, trying to funnel more light into the sutures to compensate for the stalled mechanical pump.

Your body is trying to de-frag by inducine hypometabolism to avoid any more deuterium collection. Your body is trying to "cool the stator" to prevent the brain from overheating due to the blocked exhaust.

The Action Plan (Moving the Needle)

**B. Optimize the "Dual Exhaust"**

Cold Thermogenesis (CT) + Sun: You're doing this, but you need to do CT on the face and neck specifically. Submerging the face in ice water triggers the Mammalian Dive Reflex, which "shocks" the 4th ventricle and forces a Vagal flush.
Targeted 0.66eV Light: Use the Sperti and NIR lamp directly on the Petrous bone (behind the ears) and the Sphenoid (temples) to "thaw" the heavy water in Meckel's Cave.

Your dryness (eyes, skin, vaginal) is Isotopic Dehydration. D2O displaces H2O in the mucus membranes.

Stop the MCT/Ghee Overload: High fat without a working vortex just adds "Inertial Mass."

If you don't "pump" while you drink DDW, the "Light Water" won't reach the brainstem. This is where the sleep issue resides.

**D. Environmental "Degaussing"**

Kansas, near a Bio-lab and Military base, is a High-nnEMF / Low-Flux Zone.

You must turn off the breaker (Great), but you needs to check for Geopathic Stress or local "Magnetic Stalls." Your area is horrible due to military and GPS installations. Kansas has the worst magnetic footprint in America right now.

The Progesterone/DHEA Stall: These hormones aren't working because the Dielectric Constant of her cells is too low (k=78). They are "floating" in heavy water and can't find the receptor. You need to ground on bare Earth (Kansas soil) specifically at Sunrise to reset the "Z-axis."

**My Decentralize Summary**

Your "Stall" is Mechanical. You have the "fuel" (Seafood/Sun) but the "piston" (Jaw/Sphenoid) is seized. Until you restarts the Meckel’s Cave Tuning Fork, you will remain a "Light-Starved" Fitzpatrick 1 whose 2 AM "Glymphatic Backlog" prevents deep sleep.

# Thread: Relocating from NZ -> Chile pros and cons
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-from-nz-chile-pros-and-cons.32544/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### The.Naked.Doctor — 2026-04-12T19:43:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-from-nz-chile-pros-and-cons.32544/post-370793>

* * *

**PIHA vs SOUTHERN CHILE: would love your thoughts from folks who have lived in temperate/mountain regions.....**

Hey crew… I’d really value your input on this because it’s turned into a much bigger life decision than I expected

A bit of real context first: Im originally from the Bahamas, but have been living in PIha New Zelaand for the last decade. My father passed away recently, and I’ve now inherited part of our family farm in southern Chile near Panguipulli. So this isn’t just a fantasy, it’s a very real option that’s sitting right in front of me. My mum lives here alone and isnt moving off the mountain ha ha... shes cool though and would jsut live here alone if needed.
Before dad died, my plan was actually to **buy land in Fiji, an outer island (very cheap to build a metal roof shack and just live small** chasing the equatorial light, but let’s be real… I’m not a Bitcoin maxi, I don’t have the capital just to go out and buy some perfect off-grid paradise (so this would be a very simple life- which frankly i do love)...... Our place in NZ is currently set up this way- we do all our own building, and have a very basic place...(think composting toilets, one room - big deck- decent garden)....
SO>....What I would have in Southern Chile:

-   LOTS of Land
-   No rent
-   Food security (lots of cows, goats, ducks, water)
-   And a very biologically clean environment (no closer neighbours within a 20 min drive... and we are off grid/self-sustained)
-   Sunrise and sunset view - but can be fucking cloudy/misty mornings...in fall/winter

I’m trying to think clearly about what actually matters most.

Also for context:

-   I’m quite introverted
-   I run an online medical practice so I can work from anywhere
-   I don’t need a social scene, but…
-   i LOVE nature and especially HORSES and riding- which I can't have in Piha as ur porperty is on the side of a cliff
-   There are about 8 families around us who are incredibly solid and supportive

### **Option 1: Stay in Piha, NZ 36.9S (current base**​

**Pros:**

-   Black sand beach (IR/thermal effects) UHHHHHH LOVE THIS- i swim all year and im barefoot 99% of my life
-   Direct ocean access and nice waterfalls swims --- grounding, saltwater, horizon light
-   Incredible sunsets from our place
-   Good fishing...

**Cons:**

-   3 Big ASS 4/5G n**ew large cell towers** right in line of sight. Our house is shielded, and we have kill switches etc but man, those fuckers are strong!
-   Noticeable nnEMF increase, i.e., more tinnitus, sleep issues etc
-   Western-facing house - **not ideal for morning light - BUT I walk in the hills in the morning anyway..**
-   Winter = low UV + grey stretches
-   Increasing population density / less “wild” than it used to feel. Huge tourist influx in summer = no more naked waterfall swims

### **Option 2: Family farm in southern Chile 39.6S lat(~550m elevation)**​

**Pros:**

-   ~8 families across ~1000 hectares → extremely low density
-   Our property borders a 6,000-hectare native reserve that cannot be developed- epic horse riding, camping, hunting, trout fishing etc.
-   The 22 surrounding properties cannot be subdivided → this will stay low-density long-term
-   Basically, very little/no nnEMF on much of the land
-   Clean spring water, animals, homegrown food-- real food security
    -   We’ve got cows, ducks, and land to grow whatever we want
    -   We also have a large lagoon/pond we could stock with salmon/trout, apparently.....
-   North-facing - good access to morning light
-   Large land, I can actively track and choose light environments throughout the day
-   ~550m elevation- potentially stronger UV exposure
-   Sleeping more deeply here already
-   **No rent / no financial pressure**

Living setup:

-   I can stay in a **small cabin with minimal WiFi/exposure only if I decide to use it - no cell coverage**
-   My mum is in the main house → support there if needed, but I still get space

**Cons:**

-   No ocean (this is the hardest part for me)!!!!!!- BUT some freezing ass cold rivers on our property!!
-   Mountain weather = more variability/cloud cover- this is so far kicking my ass the most.
-   Winters may feel more isolating / even lower light consistency
-   Less stimulation vs. the coastal environment - wild winds, raging seas lots of crazy energy.
-   Magnetic field / coastal vs inland differences (Chile much lower than Piha nz)

### **The model I’m considering:**​

Use Chile as a base...
Then travel during winter (Late June-early August)
to:

-   Nornern Peru
-   El Salvador
-   Ecuador, etc

(Mum can watch the dogs.... makes this realistically doable for long stints)

### **What I’d really love input on:**​

-   Does ultra-low nnEMF + land + food security outweigh losing the ocean to you? I was born in the ocean (literally hahaha hippie parents, and lived near the ocean my whole life)...
-   For those living in mountain/rural environments:
    -   How is your circadian rhythm + mood vs coastal living?
    -   Any noticeable differences in drive/dopamine/energy?
-   Anyone living around 500–600m elevation:
    -   Do you actually feel a difference in UV/light quality?
-   Has anyone successfully built a seasonal migration lifestyle around light?

### **Where I’m at (honestly):**​

Piha is magic… but the towers feel like a long-term red flag I can’t ignore. Also, winter does suck, but while in Piha, I can only travel 2 weeks at a time to the islands due to no one being able to watch my dogs for longer...
Chile feels like

-   Safety (from an EMF + density perspective)
-   Resilience (food, water, land)
-   Regulation (nervous system, sleep) but potentially requires much more intentional care around winter light exposure, especially coming from a Bahamas-level sun requirement.

Would genuinely love your thoughts, especially from:

-   People who’ve **left the ocean**
-   Those living **rurally or in the mountains**

Muchas Gracias!  Piha vs Chile!

This discovery from **LOFAR (2025)** is the "Smoking Gun" for my thesis. It provides the **Universal Stator** that explains why the "Vortex" isn't just a local biological fluke, but a fundamental property of the cosmos from the beginning no matter what theory you believe.
If the entire universe is threaded with a single, coherent magnetic field from the "Early Universe," then everything, from a galaxy to a Godwit's pecten oculi, is a **nested fractal of this primordial magnetic circuit. It highlight just how important this SAA data link to oxygen and melanin biology is.

1\. The Universal Stator vs. The Biological Rotor**

This discovery confirms that Earth (and humans) are not "floating in a vacuum"; we are **tapped into a cosmic power line.

The Thesis Link:** My argument that a declining magnetic field (the SAA/Antarctic emissions) causes an "Isotopic Meltdown" becomes even more critical. If Earth's local field is our "transformer" for this universal field, then a "degaussing" Earth isn't just a local problem, it's a **disconnection from the Universal Source.

The Vortex logic:** Galaxies align in patterns "gravity alone cannot account for" because they are following the magnetic flux lines of this universal field. This is the **Macroscopic Vortex**. Your "CSF Vortex" in the 4th ventricle is the **Microscopic fractal** of this very same process.

**2\. Primordial Field vs. Galactic Noise**

The fact that this field was generated _before_ galaxies formed is the "Thermonuclear" part of this data:

**Isotopic Sovereignty:** It proves that **Magnetism came before Matter.** In my model, this means the "Magnetic Stator" is what organized the first protons and excluded the first Deuterium. When I wrote about the formation of melanin and heme I told you both came before code. Befroe their was code Earth was anoxic and flow. That was it.

**The "Rockefeller" Error:** Modern science thinks matter (chemicals/hormones) creates the field. This discovery proves the **Field created the Matter.** Therefore, treating a disease like T1D with chemicals (Insulin) while ignoring the magnetic disconnection is a fundamental "Thermodynamic Crime" at the core of centralized medicine.

**3\. Implications for the 5th Extinction and Beyond**

If this universal field is the "thread" of the observable universe, then life is essentially a **Magnetic Antenna**.

**The Godwit/Neanderthal Connection:** Those who can "read" and "resonate" with this primordial field survive. When Earth's field wobbles (Laschamp/Modern Declination), those without a "Melanin Shield" (like the Neanderthals or modern Blue-light-drenched humans) lose their connection to the "Universal Clock."

**The Parkinson's/Fascia Link:** Every "stiff" fascia layer and "locked" dopamine neuron is an antenna that has lost its **Coherent Resonance** with this universal field. The "concrete" (Deuterium) is what happens when the antenna gets "noisy" and can no longer "hear" the cosmic signal.

**The "Fractal Rosetta Stone"**
We now have a complete, top-down hierarchy for my thesis:

1.  **Universal Field:** The primordial Stator (LOFAR 2025).
2.  **Earth's Field:** The local Transformer (now degaussing/SAA).

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-19T01:16:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-from-nz-chile-pros-and-cons.32544/post-371462>

Here is some data to add jespada to your handout.

Absolute geomagnetic field intensities for periods before the era of direct observations (the first direct absolute measurements of intensity were made in 1832 by Gauss) can only be recovered from the paleomagnetic study of burned archeological materials and volcanic rocks that acquired in the past a thermoremanent magnetization (TRM) parallel and proportional to the Earth's magnetic field present at the moment of their cooling after firing at high temperatures.

By studying well-dated volcanic rocks or archeological features from the same region (of less than 1000 km of radius) and covering different time periods, paleosecular variation (PSV) curves can be built, which were done by Molina-Cardín et al., in 2018. In addition, these data allow developing reconstructions of the past geomagnetic field at regional or global scales (Panovska et al., 2019 and references therein). These global models extend the present and historical descriptions of the geomagnetic field back in time and contribute to the investigation not only of the field changes at the Earth's surface but also at the core-mantle boundary (CMB).

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-19T01:19:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-from-nz-chile-pros-and-cons.32544/post-371463>

**MODELING THE SAA:** In an effort to investigate intensity variability at regional and global scales, the number of studies on well-dated heated archeological remains has greatly increased during the last decade.

In particular, new archeointensities obtained for the Levant revealed a large positive anomaly (called the Levantine Iron Age Anomaly, LIAA; Shaar et al., 2016) related to exceptionally high field values and fast variations of the geomagnetic field strength between the 10th and the 8th centuries BCE and directions far from those corresponding to a Geocentric Axial Dipole field (Shaar et al., 2016).

**SO IT HAS HAPPENED TWICE RECENTLY...........

Few know this.**

In fact, the virtual axial dipole moments (VADM) recorded by archeological materials constraining the LIAA reached values close to 190 ZAm2, which is twice the axial dipole moment estimations derived from global models for the same time interval (95–100 ZAm2 according to Davies and Constable, 2017).

T**_wo geomagnetic spikes characterize the LIAA event: one at 980 BCE and the other at the beginning of the 8th century BCE (Shaar et al., 2016)._** This extreme variation of the geomagnetic field intensity around 1000 BCE, with the field rapidly decreasing by a factor of two, corresponds to one of the most prominent positive anomalies depicted **_in the recent geomagnetic field._** Despite the importance of this feature, the temporal and geographic limits of the LIAA are still poorly understood by centralized science.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-19T03:59:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-from-nz-chile-pros-and-cons.32544/post-371480>

Getting precise ground-level magnetic declination data is difficult because the Earth's magnetic field isn't a single "solid" object, it’s a chaotic, multi-layered signal. Most public data (like the World Magnetic Model) is just an **averaged mathematical guess**, not a real-time measurement.
Here is why we are "blind" on the surface. Few people know this.

**1\. The Vector Problem: We Only See "Half" the Wave**

Most surface sensors (like the ones in your phone) are 3-axis fluxgates that struggle to distinguish between the **Horizontal (H)** and **Vertical (Z)** components in a way that accounts for the **Total Intensity (F)**.

**Declination vs. Inclination:** Declination is just the horizontal angle toward "True North." But the real "action" is in the **Inclination (dip)**.

**The Missing Link:** If you only measure the horizontal component, you miss the vertical flux. In a magnetic decline event (or in the SAA), the vertical vector "wobbles" or flattens. This makes the horizontal compass reading highly unstable and "noisy," but the data reported to the public often smooths this out to maintain the illusion of a stable dipole. It is good for government business keep the natives retarded in their thinking about magentic flux on Earth

**2\. Crustal Noise vs. Core Signal**

At the surface, the "Core Signal" (the big Universal Stator) is obscured by the **Crustal Field**.

**Magnetic "Fog":** Local iron deposits, basalt, and modern **electrosmog (nnEMF)** create a "magnetic fog" at the surface.

**The SWARM Necessity:** We need the **ESA SWARM** satellites because they sit _above_the crustal noise. They can see the "clean" vector coming directly from the core dynamo. Without SWARM, we can't tell if a local decline is a global trend or just "crustal interference."

**3\. The "Slow-Motion Storm" is Non-Linear**

Geomagnetic data is usually updated in **5-year increments** (the WMM). However, non-dipole features like the LIAA/SAA move and pulse much faster than that.

**The 2025/2026 Shift:** We are currently seeing "Geomagnetic Jerks", sudden accelerations in the field's shift. Standard surface models can't keep up with these jerks, so they appear "missing" or "unavailable" in public databases until the model is retrospectively corrected.

**4\. The "One-Part" Measurement Error**

Most ground stations measure the **Intensity**, but they don't measure the **Phase Coherence**.

To my thesis: If the magnetic field is a "vortex," you need to measure the **spin/torque** of the field, not just its strength.

Current surface tech is like trying to measure a tornado with a thermometer; you get the temperature (intensity) but completely miss the "spin" (the dielectric torque) that keeps the **160THz signal** rectified.

**The "Smoking Gun" Result:** This is why El Salvador’s volcanoes are so important. They act as **natural vertical probes** that pierce the crustal noise, allowing the "Universal Stator" to ground directly into the surface.

### jespada — 2026-04-19T22:16:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-from-nz-chile-pros-and-cons.32544/post-371534>

> [Alex\_fr said:](/goto/post?id=371454)
>
> This is a great initiative thank you.
> What number do you use for Magnetism by country?

The Mag score is a 1–5 scale based on field intensity in nanotesla (nT):

┌───────┬───────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Score │ Field Intensity │ Meaning │
├───────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 5 │ >55,000 nT, outside SAA │ Strongest (Iceland, Alaska, Kamchatka, Siberia) │
├───────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 4 │ 45,000–55,000 nT, outside SAA │ Strong (Central America, NZ South Island, Tierra del Fuego) │
├───────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 3 │ 35,000–45,000 nT │ Moderate (Canaries, Sicily, Japan, Taiwan, Panama) │
├───────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 2 │ 25,000–35,000 nT │ Weak │
├───────┼───────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 1 │ <25,000 nT or inside SAA │ Critically weak / in SAA │
└───────┴───────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

The underlying raw numbers come from the NOAA World Magnetic Model 2025 and ESA Swarm satellite data (Finlay, Kloss, Gillet 2025). Earth's surface field ranges approximately 25,000–66,000 nT globally.

Key reference points from the Swarm paper:
\- Siberia max: 61,619 nT (+260 nT growth)
\- Canada max: 58,031 nT (−801 nT decline)
\- SAA minimum: 22,094 nT (−336 nT decline)

So the scores are derived from absolute nT intensity at the location's coordinates, binned into the 5 tiers above. The weighted formula then doubles the Mag score: W = 2×Mag + UVB + Pop + Vol + 2×SAA.

### jespada — 2026-04-20T16:12:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relocating-from-nz-chile-pros-and-cons.32544/post-371576>

Good catch — you were right. The Mag scores for Central America (and several other low-latitude places) were wrong. I was assigning Mag 4 to Central America, but when you look up the actual total field intensity via WMM 2025 / IGRF-14, it's ~33–36k nT — that's Mag 2–3 on my own scale, not 4.

What I fixed:
\- Central America: Mag 4 → Mag 2–3 (Costa Rica 2, El Salvador/Nicaragua/Guatemala 3)
\- Philippines: 4 → 3 (~38k nT)
\- Tierra del Fuego: 4 → 2 (~30k nT)
\- Chilean Patagonia: 4 → 2 (~30–35k nT)
\- New Zealand South Island: 4 → 5 (upgraded — actually ~55–58k nT)

Why Central America stays Tier 1 despite the lower Mag score: it's the only region on the planet that simultaneously hits UVB 5 + Volcano 5 + Anti-SAA 5. No other place converges all three. The raw total drops from 22–23 to 21, but the tier status holds on the strength of those other factors.

I also added per-factor breakdowns for all Tier 4 entries (same score-bar format as Tiers 1–3), plus an explanatory note above the Tier 1 table making the UVB+Vol+SAA convergence explicit.

This is the **"Magnetic Arctic Flashover."** While the media focuses on "warming," I'm identifying the **Isotopic Phase Shift** of the planet. The arrival of mosquitoes in Iceland is the biological proof that the **Z-axis vortex** of the Arctic has stalled, allowing the **"Maillard-Orange" entropy** of the tropics to migrate North.

In my framework, this is the **Reverse-Cambrian Migration**:

**1\. The Arctic as the "Cranial Vault" of Earth**
In the planetary Lagrangian, the Arctic is the **"Brain."** It should be the site of high magnetic intensity, low entropy, and high-dielectric "ice-lattice" water.

**The Stall:** As the dynamo slows and **Magnetic Declination** jerks westward, the Arctic is losing its **Magnetic Pin. This is why the Magnetic north pole is running from Canada to Siberia at record rates.

The Result:** The "viscosity" of the Arctic atmosphere and ocean is collapsing. Without the magnetic stator to keep the cold "Z-axis" intact, the **Thermohaline "Vagus"** (the AMOC) stalls. This allows the high-entropy, deuterium-heavy air from the "Red Spot" (the SAA/Equator) to flood the North.

**2\. Arthropods: The Environmental "Hall Effect" Sensors**

Mosquitoes are not "reacting to heat"; they are **detecting a change in the Magnetic Vector.

The High-Hertz Antenna:** Arthropods are covered in **chitinous hair-antennae** (like the ones we discussed in humans). These are highly sensitive to **UPE (light leakage)** and magnetic gradients. Chitin is highly piezoelectric.

**The Migration:** Mosquitoes thrive in high-deuterium, stagnant (low-dielectric) water. Their arrival in Iceland is the "Canary in the Coal Mine" that the Icelandic water table has become massively **Deuterated. Normally mosquitos were kept out of Iceland by its very high magetic fields. Now that it is collapsing the Universal Stator cannot keep them out.** The "Universal Stator" has frayed enough that Iceland is now an **Isotopic Match** for the tropics. This is also explains why the thermohaline current in the north Atlantic is collapsing at the same time.

**3\. The 4x "Warming" Speed: The Dielectric Breakdown**

The Arctic "warms" faster because it is the **Grounding Plate** for the Earth's magnetic circuit.

**The Friction:** When a magnetic field weakens, the **Intersystem Crossing (ISC)** in the atmosphere fails. Free radicals stay in the **Singlet State**, generating massive **ROS and Heat** (entropy) instead of stable "Triplet" repair. This is why the Arctic is melting.

**The Flashover:** This is a planetary **Maillard Reaction.** The Arctic is "browning" because its magnetic insulation is gone. This is why the "warming" is non-linear, it’s a **Dielectric Breakdown**, not a slow "greenhouse" effect.

**4\. The "Misnomer" of Climate Change**

### Mauka7 — 2026-04-20T00:52:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-living-in-hawaii-for-40-years.32548/post-371543>

Born November 7, 1956. Oshkosh, Wisconsin 44.0277 Latitude -88.5477 Longitude.

mDNA - H7

Father’s side from UK. Died 56yrs

Mother’s side from Germany, Czech area, other records lost in WW2?

Had seven children, outlived husband by 20 years. Died 73yrs.

Maternal grandmother. Died 70’s.

Maternal great grandmother. Had nineteen children, outlived husband by 15 yrs.

Age died?

Lived in Oshkosh first 20 yrs. Typical SAD diet. Father hunted so we had occasional wild game. Outside a lot when young; deep tans. Tv came along. Watched every night after supper. Very bad sore throats in winter. Ongoing nasty excretion from tonsils like cauliflower (commercial dairy?). Plantar’s warts surgically removed from toes after high school.

Lived in Gainesville, Florida next ten years. Drifted into vegetarianism (mainly lacto-veg). Attended community collage – graphic design, then University of Florida – fine arts. No car, so rode bicycle everywhere. Peritonsillar abscess (my left side) 23rd birthday (lots of dairy and peanut butter). Repeated on Thanksgiving. Hospitalized five days. On antibiotics for six weeks. Tonsils removed late December. Immune system wrecked. Depression.

Worked as graphic artist for utility company; old style cut and paste. Desktop publishing came in and my job was on the way out. Was working on T-shirt designs with a friend. He moved to Hawaii. I followed later.

After a rough start, first 20 yrs on the windward side of the Big Island were okay. T-shirt project didn’t work out. Worked on a banana farm,
then delivered tofu around the island. Driving was killing my back. 2000 found work at a little natural food store in Keaau. Had a cyst from semimembranosus tendon removed from behind left knee. Started getting basal skin cancer: first shoulder below right clavicle, then cheek,
then bridge of nose ( had Mohs treatment). Another spot on my left nostril started. Did not want to go through Mohs again. My boss mentioned the Eggplant Cure. Got Curaderm and successfully treated area.

2008 changed everything. Kilauea volcano started kicking out 10x the amount of lava and gases. Began having sinus, respiratory problems – pneumonia 2014 and 2024. Rashes, especially right armpit and right ear tenderness (overload from head draining into right thoracic lymph duct? Varicose veins stripped 2015?

The worst vog symptoms start as a tightness in sinuses, then headache, stiff neck, stiff back, gut tightens (probably transverse colon), finally restless left leg. Affects me more at night. Massage does nothing. I do back twists, use Ma roller, sip a magnesium citrate drink, do a progressive relaxation back from anus. Intensity of symptoms varies dependent I suppose on active eruption, amount of off gassing, and whats coming out at given time. I know this also happens during the day as my tendons seem to tighten up and everything snap, crackles, and pops. I get sudden onset irritability. Was tested for food, mold, etc. Tried raw vegan for a while. Lots of fruit (deuterium bombs). Ended because B12 fell through floor, started getting tremor in right hand (there is Parkinson’s on my mom’s side). Triglycerides sky rocketed; too much fructose in the liver.

I worked during Covid - I did not get the jab. Took Vit D, Quercetin, Vit C, zinc/copper and Ivermectin. Sixth months after pandemic declared over I stopped taking that protocol and a sick ex-employee breezed through and three of us got it. Bad headache, no respiratory issues, but really severe nausea. Endless dry heaves. Couldn’t even drink water, or take Ivermectin. Emergency room, anti-nausea medication. Got home took Ivermectin, well in a few days, but tired for weeks.

At beginning of Covid started Keto; not very clean. Then started adding fish and eggs in. Did way too much Half-n-half, then cut entirely. (colitis) Ate local beef. Too much of a change. Now I’m eating mainly tilapia and canned mackerel, local eggs, a little local goat cheese. I eat some of the fruit and greens from my property. We have some taro and wild yams too. I think of Jeremy Thomley and wonder if I’ll need to eat more carbs. Been told I have COPD, lost 20% lung function. Get 93 reading Walmart oximeter; if that means anything.

Just to mention, kidney function is low, fasting insulin is too low. White blood cell count tends to be high.

I can see how switching to an indoor environment at work, along everyone’s phone (don’t have a ‘smart’ phone), stressors like Covid and all the political stuff at work, my age, may have contributed to current health status. Cell tower at the shopping center I work at. Cell tower less than 2 miles from my home. Watched Dvd’s with supper at night for years. Got a laptop in 2005. Who doesn’t like to surf Youtube? Now with the magnetic part added in, Crocs don’t seem like a good Idea either.

Crammed a lot in here. Next post I’ll look at what I’m changing in my lifestyle.

### Mauka7 — 2026-05-01T03:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-living-in-hawaii-for-40-years.32548/post-372702>

I thought of a few more health symptoms or indicators not mentioned in previous post.

Around the time of the colitis got ringworm on the instep of both feet and industrial strength dandruff. Was able to stop the ringworm with gentian violet, nothing else worked. The dandruff still persists.

After the vein removal tried compression stockings for a while. Didn’t work; big feet and skinny ankles stretched the socks and rubbed the hair off lower leg. (Or is it really because of insulin issues?)

I had been drinking a turmeric tea for a while, so my teeth had a yellow cast. (Phosphorous issue?) Maybe some Dupuytren on palms. On/off timnnitis.

Over the last six months I’ve gotten up for sunrise. On days off will sit in net tent til around 9am, then work on the land. Switched back to incandescent (40wat + a red bulb). Wear amber safety glasses after sunset. Put a piece of orange stained glass over computer screen. No devices after dark.

Put carbon plugs in my Crocs (high tech), there are now holes in the soles (low tech). Have a copper impregnated piece of cloth connected to a wire out the window to ground for sleeping. Turn off the power to my one room efficiency cabin at night. The electric refrigerator is run by extension cord. It is eight feet from the bed.

Am eating frozen tilapia or canned mackerel with wakame seaweed and coconut meat for breakfast

(half hour after sunrise). Some fruit from my property during the day. Local poached eggs and greens, or quiche, or steamed veggies with ghee or local goat cheese for supper (aim for 4pm). Drinking more water (sometimes Icelandic if I can afford it). DDW for an extended time is way beyond my means at the moment. Sometimes will have coconut water, but chill it and drink mid-day while sweating out in the gardens. Started chewing gum to deplete deuterium. (Spry w/ Xylitol)

Did some crude CT on knees for a while. Two gallon jugs of frig water, combined with two trays of ice cubes in a cut off 20 gallon barrel. Ladled the cold water over knees for 15 minutes, then poured it over the rest of body.

I have noticed some improvement in my knees. They right knee now is about the same size as the left knee. It still does not have the range of motion as the left, but both have more. There is less stiffness and pain.

I became a gold member and am working on the half-hour consultation fee. Re-reading PaleoRx.

> [@Tomasz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/42387/) - out of curiosity how do you feel there in comparison to where you moved from? We had planned for 6 months around mission beach / Atherton area.

Moving here got rid of my "palindromic arthritis' that I had when I lived in the Blue Mountains. However, I was working nights and would have been vitamin D deficient. It is beautiful up here. The volcanic crater lakes Lake Eacham and Barrine are stunning and have a good feel to them. If considering the Tablelands, I would look around those areas. Happy to chat about it or if you're up this way let me know and I can show you around.

Uncle Jack has explained how plain old baking soda (sodium bicarbonate) from your comatose beta cells that are latice locked can act like an "all-clear" signal for your body's cells by de-fragging your beta cells.

Your cells have an "emergency mode" called Cell Danger Response (CDR) that ramps up inflammation when stressed (bad food, toxins, heavy exercise). It helps short-term but drags on and causes issues like gut slowdown or fatty liver.

Baking soda helps by: - Flushing "heavy hydrogen" (deuterium) junk from cells that fogs up signals. - Boosting acetylcholine (a chill-out chemical) to tell your spleen/immune system: "Crisis over, relax."

\- Restoring natural bicarb your pancreas makes (disrupted by some diabetes/weight-loss drugs like GLP-1s).

Athletes use it to cut muscle acid and recover faster (it's why it's banned in horse racing as "milk shaking").

To take advantage safely: Don't self-dose without a doc/parent. Some folks mix 1/4 tsp in water before workouts or meals for gut/liver support, but test small and monitor.

Focus on basics too—sleep 8+ hrs, cut late-night screens, eat real food.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-13T17:10:12+0300

### Rusty — 2026-04-13T18:22:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunlight-on-cloudy-days.32551/post-370918>

**On a clear sunny day, around noon,**

you receive around **800-1000 W/m²** solar power.

This is when the sun is at its highest point and the intensity of the sunlight is strongest.

**When you are under the shade of a tree**

you receive around **80-200 W/m²** solar power.

Roughly around 10% to 20% of the direct sunlight

**With Thin or light cloud cover**

you receive about 50% to 70% of the direct sunlight.

This corresponds to roughly **400 to 700 W/m²

With Moderate Overcast**

you receive about 30% to 50% of the direct sunlight.

This translates to roughly **240 to 500 W/m²

With Heavy Overcast**

Under heavy overcast conditions, the sunlight is further reduced to about 10% to 30% of the direct sunlight.

This means you are still getting roughly 80 to 300 W/m² solar irradiance

**tl;dr**

When the sun is out, you should be out too.

Doesn't matter if you are in shade or if there are clouds

YOU.

ARE.

STILL.

GETTING.

THE.

BENEFITS.

# Thread: New Gold Member - Question for Dr. Kruse

### JimBo99 — 2026-04-13T23:11:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-gold-member-question-for-dr-kruse.32554/post-370945>

Hi Doc, Just upped my game to Gold after reading your forum and Patreon over the last 2 years. 66yo, and I live in remote central Idaho away from most NnEMF, get morning sunrise/midday/sunset sun for 9 months, then go south for 3 months in winter. Vit D levels hover btwn 50-70 yearround w/ no pills. About 15 years ago I developed tinnitus overnight after the following circumstances: I had to perform firearm qualifications that involved discharging 50 rounds each of a 30 cal rifle, 12 ga shotgun, and 9mm handgun. I wore earplugs under good earmuff for all shots. That night, I heard ringing, but thought, "I've been to loud concerts in my day.. this too will pass." It didn't - went to an audiologist 2 weeks later, and he found hearing loss btwn 4500-5000 Hz and told me I had physically damaged my hair cells in my earws responsible for sending signals to my brain. Also told me that even though I used ear pro, it only deadened the resulting sound transmitted through my skull to my ear 20 db or so, and I probably experienced 140 db percussive impulses to my inner ear. I still have bad ringing all the time, and while I've read about your various explanations on your blogs, tweets, and Forum that tinnitus is caused by deficits in light/water/magnetism, I think my condition is different. Can you give me any guidance on what I can do to help it? Am willing to do a consult if necessary. Much appreciated, Thanks, Jim

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-13T23:19:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-gold-member-question-for-dr-kruse.32554/post-370947>

Your audiologist or ENT need to figure out if you have senisneural or boneconductive loss in the band. That will make the advice easy. A proper neuro exam of the ear would tell that,

### JimBo99 — 2026-04-13T23:29:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-gold-member-question-for-dr-kruse.32554/post-370949>

I was told I had bone conductive loss of the inner hair cells arranged along the basilar membrane. The hearing loss band of ~4khz was to be expected for my type of "noise-induced hearing loss." He mentioned something about the stria vascularis also, but was a long time ago, so details evade me. I tried "smart" hearing aids about 8 years ago that "filled in" for the lost frequencies, but after I realized the devices were talking to each other through my skull, I quit them after 2 weeks. I wasn't savvy about nNEMF back then. Of course, the fear that your prompted with by centralized medicine is, that if you don't treat your hearing loss, you will suffer cognitive decline, so I wasted $2,500 for nothing.

From what I've found, for my tinnitus condition, the Nurosym/Nuropod is preferable, altho 2x as expensive.
**What the Parasym/Nurosym device is**

Parasym delivers Auricular Vagal Neuromodulation Therapy (AVNT) — targeted electrical stimulation applied via the auricular branch of the vagus nerve at the ear, providing direct access to key brain regions involved in autonomic regulation, inflammation control, and stress processing. The device has been validated in 50+ completed scientific trials, studied in partnership with Harvard, UCLA, and other leading institutions, and is considered by many researchers as the gold standard for non-prescription ear-based vagal stimulation.

In the US it's sold as the Nuropod; in Europe and elsewhere it's the Nurosym — same technology, different branding, launched in the US market in September 2025.

# Thread: Rapid advancing cataracts
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rapid-advancing-cataracts.32555/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Borja — 2026-04-13T23:40:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rapid-advancing-cataracts.32555/post-370951>

This is the case of my mother so I did not know if put it here or in the my optimal journey.

She is 65 and has always lived near Barcelona (41°N), 10km far away from the airport.
About 25 years ago they removed 3/4 of her thyroid gland due to a nodule. Since then taking Levotiroxina 50mg. All this time T3 and T4 have been in the accepted ranges of centralized medicine.
She got the AstraZeneca jabs.
A few years ago she got detected very slight cataracts.

A couple of years ago she started to use hearing aids, which may be used directly as headphones, so they are always blasting bluetooth. Also she used the Bluetooth a lot.
Some months ago she got checked because did not see well. Cataracts have advanced very quickly. In one eye she sees only 60%, in the other 80%.

She wears glasses with metal frame.

After reading the blog on cataracts some weeks ago, and seeing a Simulation of metal frame glasses that [@Rusty](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26197/) put on twitter with metal frame glasses, it is no wonder cataracts advanced so quickly.

She also tells me she has dry skin and insomnia.

For the last 6 months she installed red lights almost everywhere and wears blue blockers at night.

She has a beach appartment a bit further away from Barcelona so I told her to:
\- Do not use the hearing aids.
\- Move to the beach appartment (10-15km away from airport)
\- See the sunrise and stay at the beach grounded as long as possible.
\- Get new glasses without any UV/blue filter and with plastic frame.
\- Eat as much seafood as possible.
\- Try not to use much technology.

So far, standard decentralized procedure. Here come the doubts:

\- I told her to get DDW so she ordered today Qlarivia 25ppm. **Is it recommended to mix it with regular water to achieve, say, a 100ppm water? Or should she go all in and drink only 25ppm? And also, should she hydrate more than normal, or just drink to thirst? Always with a little bit of salt for better absortion?

She also has a red lamp I gave her with 630, 670, 727, 810 and 850nm. Should she use it? If so, what protocol to use?**

Here some more questions that come to mind:

As long as I understand this is a redox problem partly or fully caused by electromagnetism. Reduced CCO productivity --> reduced water --> reduced heat sink (poor UPEs) --> brain does not want that --> hello cataracts.
What about using cold masks or "eye cold plunges" to cool the eyes now that she lacks metabollic water? Is it useless because is only for a short period of time? Or is it worth a try? If so, any idea on how often and how long to do it?
And using (maybe cool) DDW as eye drops?
I guess guetting in the sea with the eyes under water would be great. But if she does not do that, is it worth it to lie down at the beach with eyes directly on the sand to ground near the eye, say, 30min daily?

I find it interesting that even with the redox problem, during the last year she was actually the most tanned in a while. It seems counterintuitive to me.

Any comment is appreciated!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
